attacks.mechanism: In a sustained blackout where your vouching contacts drift out of range, your budget lapses and you cannot relay or inject — a liveness failure precisely when the system matters most. Honest people get starved while a dense farm with co-located vouchers stays funded.

candidates.name: WITNESSED SWEAT — Co-Presence-Witnessed Sequential Work, minted into self-verifying KVAC tokens ("Proof-of-Strangers-and-Sweat")
candidates.angle: WILDCARD-NOVEL hybrid. The mint price is two things a co-located device farm cannot cheaply forge together: (a) diverse human co-presence measured as a shared, uncloneable ambient-RF secret with a *distinct stranger* (ProxiMate/Amigo-style channel-reciprocity key from multipath fading — secure even against an unbounded adversary not physically between the two antennas), and (b) wall-clock-bound *sequential* memory-hard work (Argon2id-as-PoSW / VDF-flavored) seeded by that shared secret. Neither alone is enough: RF co-presence alone is farmable by stacking radios on a table; sequential work alone is just PoW (one rich actor wins). Their PRODUCT is the scarce resource. The cryptographic twist is that tokens are NOT issued by anyone: they are KVAC (algebraic-MAC / CMZ-style) credentials self-MAC'd under an epoch key that EVERY honest device can independently reconstruct from the public 'co-presence beacon soup', so verification is keyed-verification with a key the verifier already holds — no issuer, no internet, no global ledger.
candidates.howMintingWorks: DRAMATIS: a "witness encounter" between two phones A,B that are physically near (<~1-3m) for a short contact. STEP 1 (co-presence secret, ~1-2s): A and B each sample their ambient RF channel (BLE RSSI/CSI fluctuation, neighboring-AP beacon timing, mic/IMU optional) and run reciprocal-channel key agreement + fuzzy extractor (ProxiMate/Amigo). Output: a shared 128-bit secret s_AB that an attacker NOT physically between the two antennas cannot reproduce; it is fresh per encounter (multipath is time-varying). Crucially A and B each contribute a self-chosen random nonce mixed into s via a commit-then-reveal so neither can fully pre-choose it. STEP 2 (proof of a DISTINCT stranger): each side derives a per-encounter "partner tag" pt = H(s_AB || epoch). A keeps a small Bloom/HLL sketch of partner tags seen this session; an encounter only counts toward minting if pt is NOVEL in the sketch (you don't earn from re-meeting the same neighbor — kills the two-phone loop). STEP 3 (sequential sweat): the token seed is z = H(s_AB || A_eph || B_eph || epoch_beacon). Each phone independently computes a memory-hard SEQUENTIAL puzzle w = Argon2id-chain(z, t_iters, m_KiB) — parameters tuned so one phone needs ~15-60s of single-core, ~64-256MiB to finish. This is the wall-clock cost; it is per-encounter and per-seed so it cannot be amortized across many tokens. STEP 4 (self-MAC into a KVAC token): the EPOCH KEY x_epoch is a symmetric MAC key that every honest device derives deterministically from the public daily "co-presence beacon" (see serverless section) — so it is NOT secret to an issuer, it is shared-knowledge among participants. The minted token is T = KVAC.MAC(x_epoch ; attributes = {nonce u (random, the token's unlinkable serial), epoch e}) — concretely a CMZ/algebraic-MAC tag (a couple of group elements) computed by the minter itself using x_epoch, NOT a blind signature from a server. The puzzle output w is folded into the MAC binding (the token only verifies if a valid w accompanies the FIRST presentation's ZK proof) so a token can't be minted without paying the sweat. SPENDING: in a BLE relay handshake, the spender presents a randomized show of T (re-randomized MAC + a ZK proof of knowledge of a valid MAC over a fresh serial u, CMZ-style O(1) presentation) — the relaying neighbor verifies with the SAME x_epoch it already holds, and records u in its local per-session spent-set to enforce single-use. No identity, no issuer contacted, two group exponentiations to verify.
candidates.sybilResistance: R1: A single actor cannot cheaply mint unlimited tokens because each token costs (i) a NOVEL co-presence secret with a partner whose tag hasn't been seen, AND (ii) ~tens of seconds of single-core memory-hard wall-clock work seeded by that exact secret. The sequential+memory-hard puzzle resists ASIC/GPU/parallel speedup (Argon2id's S·T >= n^2/1536 lower bound; sequential chaining defeats parallelism), so throwing cores at it does not help past one-puzzle-per-core, and 256MiB working sets cap how many puzzles one cheap device runs concurrently. Minting rate per device is therefore physically bounded to roughly (cores / puzzle_time) AND (novel-strangers / time). To get N tokens you need either N*puzzle_time of compute OR N distinct human encounters — both costly, and you need BOTH together because the puzzle seed is the co-presence secret. Tokens are cheap to VERIFY (KVAC show = O(1) group ops) but expensive to MINT — exactly the asymmetry we want.
candidates.farmResistance: R2 — this is the design's whole point. A farm of M phones on one table CAN pairwise-RF-pair (they share the same room channel), so naive co-presence is farmable. Three layers stop the farm: (1) DISTINCT-STRANGER novelty: partner tags from the same M devices form a clique — at most M*(M-1)/2 distinct tags EVER, and your per-session sketch caps you at M-1 novel partners regardless of how many times you re-pair; the farm's minting is bounded by its device count, not its runtime. (2) SEQUENTIAL SWEAT: even those M(M-1)/2 seeds each still cost a full memory-hard puzzle, so a farm's throughput is (table's total cores)/(puzzle_time) — it must literally buy CPU+RAM, converting the Sybil attack into an honest PoW cost (acceptable: it's now just "you paid for the relay slots you abuse"). (3) Anti-self-pairing: the commit-reveal nonce exchange + reciprocal-channel extraction means a single phone CANNOT pair with ITSELF (no second antenna with independent multipath), and the novelty sketch means a phone re-meeting a confederate earns nothing after the first. Residual honest caveat: a farm of M genuinely-separate radios in M genuinely-different micro-locations is just M honest users — which is fine; the invariant is "tokens track real distinct co-presences," and that is what we want to meter. The farm can't beat real crowds; in a dense-gathering (R7 scope) honest users out-mint any feasible farm because they meet hundreds of genuinely-distinct strangers.
candidates.anonymityImpact: R3: Strong. The co-presence secret s_AB and the puzzle are computed locally and never transmitted; only their cryptographic derivatives go on the wire. The minted token carries a RANDOM serial u and an epoch tag e and nothing else — no identity, no link to the encounter, no link to A or B. Acquisition is unlinkable to spending because the KVAC presentation is a re-randomized show with a ZK proof (CMZ multi-show... actually single-show here: each token is single-use, but the SHOW is unlinkable to the MINT and to other tokens — fresh randomization each presentation). The token lives in the BLE HANDSHAKE, never in the blob, so the message soup stays uniform (R3/invariant-3 preserved). Caveat to be honest: the *act of pairing* is observable to someone with an RF sniffer co-located with you (they see two phones did a co-presence handshake) — but that reveals only "an encounter happened here," not who, not what message, and it is indistinguishable from cover encounters if devices periodically run cover pairings. Spending a token in a handshake is uniform with cover-token spends. So: no identity leaks; the only metadata is "co-presence happened," which is inherent to being in a crowd and is exactly the cover.
candidates.serverlessOffline: R4/R5: No issuer, no internet, no global clock, no convergence. The one thing that smells like 'global state' — the epoch MAC key x_epoch — is constructed WITHOUT a server: x_epoch is derived from a "co-presence beacon" that is a coarse, self-organizing local consensus value, e.g. x_epoch = H(epoch_id || canopy) where epoch_id is a coarse self-claimed day-bucket (NOT a synchronized clock: a few-hours skew is tolerated by accepting a small window of adjacent epoch keys, like TOTP drift windows) and 'canopy' is a low-entropy ambient public value any co-present device can sample (e.g., the set of strong long-lived BLE/AP MAC-hashes audible in this gathering, robustly sketched). KEY POINT: verification is KEYED-verification — the verifier already holds x_epoch because it derived it the same way from the same shared environment, so no issuer key distribution and no online check is needed; this is the property that makes KVAC (vs. public blind sigs) the right primitive here. Epoch rotation gives self-destruct alignment with invariant 5: when the epoch passes, old x_epoch is dropped, all tokens minted under it become unverifiable and self-shred — no revocation list, no global convergence. There is NO global ledger of spent tokens: double-spend is caught only LOCALLY per neighbor per session (a neighbor's own spent-set), which is exactly the threat model the relay-metering needs (R5: no global state). Total blackout: everything above uses only local radio sampling and local CPU — fully functional with zero infrastructure.
candidates.incentiveEffect: R7: Net-positive and the cleverest second-order effect. Tokens are the fuel you spend to GET YOUR OWN messages relayed (each relay slot costs a token). You EARN tokens by encountering distinct strangers. In a dense gathering, walking around / mingling = minting. So the act that produces relay capacity (being physically present and mobile in the crowd, i.e., being a good DTN carrier node) is the same act that mints tokens — it aligns 'be a useful mobile relay' with 'earn the right to send.' Free-riders who sit still and never meet new people slowly run out of tokens and can't flood; active carriers accumulate tokens and can both send and afford to relay. This directly counters the relay tragedy-of-the-commons: relaying is rate-limited (anti-abuse) while MOBILITY+PRESENCE is rewarded (pro-carry). Honest caveat: it mildly taxes pure stationary relays (a phone in a pocket of someone standing still), so we let RECEIVING/relaying also passively top up a small token trickle from each novel encounter even without active minting, so good stationary relays aren't starved.
candidates.buildability: R6: Buildable but the heaviest of the candidates — be honest. RF co-presence key agreement (ProxiMate/Amigo, channel-reciprocity + fuzzy extractor) is published and runs on commodity BLE/Wi-Fi RSSI in ~1-2s; CSI is better but not exposed on all phones, so v1 uses RSSI-fluctuation + beacon-timing entropy (lower key rate, needs error-correction reconciliation — real engineering risk, accept ~modest false-pair rate, retry on failure). Argon2id is RFC-9106, ubiquitous libs; a 64-256MiB / ~20-40s puzzle is a real but tolerable battery hit (it's per-NOVEL-encounter, not continuous, and can be scheduled when charging or rate-capped to e.g. a few mints/hour). KVAC/CMZ algebraic-MAC issuance+show is a few group exponentiations on a standard prime-order curve (Ristretto255) — show/verify in low ms, tokens are a few hundred bytes — cheap on the wire and verify-side. Tolerant of partitions/short contacts: minting is a fire-and-forget local computation after a brief contact (the encounter only needs the ~1-2s RF sample; the sweat finishes asynchronously afterward), and spending needs only the 2-message handshake. Biggest build risks, stated plainly: (i) reliable reciprocal-RF key extraction on heterogeneous phones is the make-or-break and may need a calibration/cover-pairing fallback; (ii) battery from memory-hard puzzles bounds mint rate (a feature for R1, a UX cost); (iii) deriving a stable enough 'canopy' for x_epoch across a partitioned gathering needs a robust sketch + adjacent-epoch acceptance window.

attacks.title: Antenna-diversity self-farm (one operator, M real radios in M spots)
attacks.mechanism: An operator distributes M cheap radios across a venue so each gets genuinely distinct multipath and meets distinct strangers/each-other, harvesting up to M*(M-1)/2 + (strangers) seeds and paying M-fold the CPU. This is the dominant residual attack. Mitigation/honest framing: it degrades into honest PoW + honest presence — the attacker is now physically occupying M locations and buying M devices' CPU; cost scales with abuse. It does NOT yield unlimited cheap tokens. In R7-scope crowds, honest users still out-mint it.

attacks.title: Relay-MITM on co-presence (wormhole)
attacks.mechanism: An adversary with antennas at two locations relays RF in real time to make two far-apart phones believe they are co-present, manufacturing 'distinct stranger' seeds. ProxiMate-class schemes assume the attacker is NOT between the antennas; a fast wormhole breaks that. Mitigation: distance-bounding round-trip timing on the BLE exchange (light), requiring multi-modal co-presence (RF + IMU/audio correlation), and the novelty sketch caps yield. Lowers but doesn't fully kill it.

attacks.title: Epoch-key (canopy) grinding / spoofing
attacks.mechanism: x_epoch depends on a low-entropy 'canopy' (ambient MAC-hash sketch + coarse day-bucket). An attacker who can predict/forge the canopy can self-MAC valid tokens WITHOUT any co-presence (because KVAC keys are symmetric/shared, anyone holding x_epoch can mint). This is the deepest weakness: the whole 'serverless self-issuance' trick means the MAC key is not secret, so token UNFORGEABILITY does NOT come from key secrecy — it comes ONLY from the puzzle+co-presence binding enforced at presentation. If a verifier ever accepts a show WITHOUT re-checking a fresh-enough co-presence/puzzle witness, forgery is trivial. Mitigation: bind every FIRST-spend to a verifiable puzzle witness w over the live epoch seed and rate-limit accept; treat x_epoch only as a namespacing/anti-cross-epoch-replay tag, NOT as the security root. Must be designed very carefully or it collapses to 'no Sybil resistance at all.'

attacks.title: Puzzle precomputation / outsourcing
attacks.mechanism: Attacker precomputes many puzzles ahead of an epoch, or rents cloud CPU to mass-produce w's, then floods. Mitigation: seed z must include the live epoch beacon (unpredictable until the epoch) AND the fresh co-presence secret, so precompute is bounded to within-epoch and still needs a real co-presence seed per token; cloud CPU just makes it honest PoW (cost-scaling).

attacks.title: Double-spend across disjoint neighbors
attacks.mechanism: Because spent-sets are LOCAL per-neighbor-per-session (no global ledger, by R5), the same token can be spent once to each of many different neighbors. Mitigation: this is acceptable by design — the metering is per-neighbor-per-session relay budget R, so a token spent to neighbor X and neighbor Y just buys one slot at each, which is the intended granularity. It does NOT amplify a single mint into unlimited GLOBAL relays because each hop still charges the relayer's own budget.

attacks.title: Cover-pairing exhaustion / battery DoS
attacks.mechanism: To keep co-presence handshakes uniform with cover, devices run cover pairings; an attacker spams pairing requests to drain victims' battery via forced Argon2 puzzles. Mitigation: minting (the expensive puzzle) is purely the local device's CHOICE after an encounter, never triggered by the peer; cover pairings are cheap (no puzzle), so an attacker cannot force a victim to compute puzzles.

ranking.name: Witnessed Relay Reciprocity (WRR)
ranking.strengths: Only candidate claiming preserves-all, and the claim holds: tokens live in the BLE handshake, never the blob, cover runs the identical relay/DAck/mint/spend loop, so soup + handshake stay uniform (inv 2/3/4). Best R7 by far - the token source IS relaying others' NOVEL traffic to DIVERSE peers, the exact behavior the mesh needs; near the percolation cliff price->0 so relaying is cheap to monetize precisely when relays are scarce, directly attacking the relay tragedy-of-the-commons. Best R4/R5 fit: local sliding epochs (own monotonic counter), no global clock, no token tree, double-spend caught purely per-neighbor-per-session via cuckoo set - exactly the §9 granularity. Strong stacked R2: concave diversity gate starves the co-located clique (saturates at clique size, sublinear return), novelty requirement zeroes recycled-blob loops, density-adaptive price self-defeats radio-packing, VDF floor caps per-device rate. Lowest build cost (R6): piggybacks on infrastructure spec v0.3 already mandates (§8 reconciliation carries DAcks, §9 VDF, §6 cuckoo filters); verify is one VDF-verify + one cuckoo lookup.
ranking.weaknesses: R1 only partial-to-good: per-device floor leans on a VDF the candidate specifies only loosely ('a few hundred ms Wesolowski squarings'), and class-group VDFs are awkward on phones (no fast class-group arithmetic) - the weakest-specified primitive in an otherwise tight design. Farm story is DETECTION-based not surface-elimination: the spread-out farm at true crowd density (K real radios at K real locations, each serving distinct strangers) passes diversity+novelty+price and is indistinguishable from honest heavy users - the unfixable residual. Fair-exchange of receipts is impossible without a TTP (Pagnia-Gaertner); bounded to one-blob theft by gradual swap but not closeable. DAck-intensity side channel needs mandatory fixed-R padding. Liveness gap: in a SPARSE crowd you can't earn receipts, so honest users with few contacts can't mint (partially mitigated by price->0 but you still need SOME novel deliveries).

ranking.name: Hourglass (PoSW + ZK nullifier)
ranking.strengths: The ONLY candidate that ELIMINATES the farm surface rather than detecting farms (best R2 mechanism in spirit): tokens are minted by compute alone with no pairing/attestation/co-location step, so a 100-phone farm in one room = 100 tokens/hr = identical to 100 phones worldwide; co-location confers literally zero advantage and there is no self-dealing loophole because mint has no relationship to relaying. Best acquisition anonymity (R3): minting is purely local computation touching no peer/network, emitting NOTHING on the wire - cannot be observed or correlated at all. Cleanest per-device physics cap (R1): SEQUENTIAL work can't be parallelized away, so one device mints ~1 token/hr regardless of cores or money - strictly better than parallelizable memory-hard PoW. Excellent R4/R5: self-certifying proof, no issuer, precompute defeated by binding nullifier to a per-session handshake anchor (no beacon, no clock). Mint primitive is trivially buildable (hash-only DAG labeling + Merkle, no class groups, no trusted setup, no group of unknown order) and verify is sub-ms - the right hardware fit for phones with SHA/BLAKE.
ranking.weaknesses: R1 has a hard ceiling the candidate admits as 'high': sequentiality caps PER-DEVICE rate but NOT device count, so botnet/cloud renting 10k sequential cores = 10k tokens/hr; reduces Sybil to 'how much sequential compute can you rent,' beatable by money (just less efficiently than parallel PoW). Worst R7: compute is decoupled from relaying, so it does NOT rebuild reciprocity - a pure compute-minter can mint then refuse to relay; the rebate fix risks reintroducing a mint-by-relaying farm loophole. Heaviest crypto-correctness risk (R6, rated 'high'): the ZK SPEND wrapper must prove 'PoSW.Verify accepts over an opening of com' in zero-knowledge (a hash relation), needs STARK-friendly hashing inside the PoSW and is the part least like off-the-shelf code - a soundness bug forges/re-spends tokens with no compute. Heterogeneous-hardware calibration of N (budget vs flagship phone) has no fix without a forbidden global difficulty oracle - accept a constant-factor advantage to the fastest serial hardware.

ranking.name: Proof-of-Encounter (KVAC mutual co-sign)
ranking.strengths: Strong R7 (earn by being present and meeting people, which you do anyway at a protest/stadium) and strong R3 via KVAC blind issuance + multi-show-unlinkable presentation (production-proven family, Signal group credentials). Excellent R4/R5: every node is its own KVAC issuer AND verifier of its own key (symmetric keyed-verification is the exact reason KVAC fits offline vs Privacy Pass); double-spend is local per-neighbor-per-session. Diversity-weighted saturating novelty sketch genuinely caps the closet-farm to ~K_div distinct peers regardless of encounter count. KVAC core is cheap (sub-ms verify, ~100-200B tokens).
ranking.weaknesses: Leans on distance-bounding for its wormhole defense; BLE-only ToF is genuinely weak (NCC Group: BLE proximity auth is relay-bypassable), so the phantom-peer defense degrades to 'weak' on non-UWB phones (rated 'high'). Mobile festival-walker farm (one human, K_div phones through a real crowd) mints K_div x normal and is NOT stopped by diversity gating because peers are genuinely novel - only out-of-scope PHY-fingerprint correlation could catch it (rated 'high'). Sketch-poisoning via per-contact commitment rotation is only partially defended. Bends one invariant. Co-sign refusal needs a fair-exchange step. Tuning the decaying counting-Bloom sketch (window/K_div/decay) is a real operational risk.

ranking.name: Quorum-Witnessed Tokens (QWT, Coconut threshold + ambient beacon)
ranking.strengths: Threshold-blind-signature core is production-grade and cheap (Coconut/PS: verify = 2 pairings independent of k, partials are independent so no interactive round between witnesses - beats FROST for disconnected ad-hoc witnesses). Strong R3 (Coconut blindness holds even if verifier colludes with all authorities; spends re-randomized and unlinkable). Fully serverless, no DKG needed (Lagrange-in-exponent over independent keypairs), no global clock (ambient beacon replaces it), no convergence. Most NOVEL R2 idea: per-witness ambient sub-fingerprint decorrelation forces a farm to supply k independent-LOOKING witnesses, which a single chassis (near-identical CSI) fails.
ranking.weaknesses: Its R2 defenses are explicitly HEURISTIC physical-layer, NOT cryptographic hardness - the engineered multi-antenna+moving-reflector farm defeats decorrelation at engineering cost (rated 'high', the honest ceiling). Ambient fuzzy-extraction (audio+CSI) across heterogeneous phones is research-grade and the least mature component - the dominant build risk (R6). Sparse-crowd LIVENESS FAILURE: needs k willing honest witnesses in range, so the system stalls exactly when the gathering is small - the property that gives Sybil-resistance makes it fragile when it matters most. Ambient-beacon replay/prediction in low-entropy environments lets pre-minting. Relay/wormhole on the loose BLE distance-bounding round. Quorum-collusion over-issuance (k colluding reals mint freely, undetectable with no global state). Bends one invariant; anonymity-set narrows to 'present at this gathering this epoch.'

ranking.name: WITNESSED SWEAT (co-presence x sequential work, KVAC self-MAC)
ranking.strengths: Conceptually elegant product-of-two-scarcities: neither RF co-presence (farmable by stacking radios) nor sequential work (just PoW, rich actor wins) suffices alone, and the PRODUCT forces a farm to pay BOTH distinct-stranger novelty AND per-seed memory-hard wall-clock. Good R7 (mingling = minting; mobility/presence rewarded, free-riders run dry). Argon2id is RFC-9106 with ubiquitous libs; KVAC show/verify cheap. Distinct-stranger novelty sketch caps a clique to M-1 partners and folds the farm into honest PoW + honest presence.
ranking.weaknesses: Deepest weakness is STRUCTURAL and rated 'high': x_epoch is shared-knowledge (anyone holding it can self-MAC), so token unforgeability does NOT come from key secrecy - it comes ONLY from puzzle+co-presence binding re-checked at presentation. The candidate itself warns 'if a verifier ever accepts a show without re-checking a fresh puzzle witness, forgery is trivial... must be designed very carefully or it collapses to no Sybil resistance at all.' That knife-edge makes the security argument brittle. RF reciprocal-channel key extraction on heterogeneous phones is make-or-break and the candidate concedes a modest false-pair rate needing retries (R6 - heaviest overall). Wormhole on co-presence, epoch-key/canopy grinding, and puzzle precompute/outsourcing all present. Battery from per-encounter memory-hard puzzles bounds mint rate (feature for R1, UX cost). Bends one invariant.

ranking.name: VOUCH (social-graph blind-vouched relay budgets)
ranking.strengths: Strongest conceptual Sybil bound IF it held: token supply capped by real social degree, not compute/time/device-count, and no PoW shortcut. KVAC core is cheap and pairing-free (deliberately chose algebraic-MAC over BLS/Coconut). Coupling refresh to proof-of-relay gives good R7 and doubles as a farm defense (self-paired farms generate no genuine third-party relay receipts).
ranking.weaknesses: Worst invariant impact: bends-SEVERAL. sk-share compromise rated CRITICAL - issuer==verifier symmetric key means ONE compromised organizer phone mints unlimited valid tokens and breaks Sybil-resistance entirely; a shared group key is a single point of total failure utterly at odds with the serverless/no-trusted-party ethos. Acquisition materializes a social graph out-of-band (strains invariant 6: pairing now carries semantic 'I trust you to relay,' not just key exchange) and is reconstructable by phone seizure. Pairing-mint farm with one legitimate entry point is unsolved (R2 bent) - internal cross-vouching multiplies up to fan-out/depth budget. Refresh-time contact-unreachability collapse: budget lapses in a sustained blackout when vouchers drift out of range - liveness failure precisely when the system matters. Epoch-beacon partition starvation. Tokens are transferable bearer instruments (gray market in relay budget).

recommended.name: Reciprocity Hourglass: diversity-gated witnessed-relay credit, priced by a self-anchored sequential-PoSW + ZK-nullifier token (WRR spine x Hourglass primitive)
recommended.design:
SPINE = WRR (the only preserves-all candidate, best R7, lowest build cost). REPLACE WRR's loosely-specified self-VDF token-cost floor with Hourglass's hash-based sequential PoSW + ZK-nullifier as the single token primitive. One token = one (s, r, com, phi, Pi) PoSW tuple where com=Commit(s;r) and Pi proves N sequential hash steps over chi=H(com). Choose Cohen-Pietrzak hash-based PoSW (Poseidon/Rescue inside, for a cheap STARK/Bulletproof spend wrapper) over a class-group VDF: phones have hash hardware, no fast class-group arithmetic, no trusted setup, no group of unknown order. This subsumes WRR's hand-waved 'Wesolowski squarings' with a buildable hash-only primitive AND gives a per-device physics cap (sequential, un-parallelizable) that WRR's R1 lacked.

TWO MINT PATHS into the SAME token format (this is the blend's key move):
(1) SWEAT PATH (Hourglass, always available, fixes liveness): any device can self-mint a token by paying N sequential hash-steps in the background (~minutes-to-an-hour, calibrated). Emits nothing on the wire (best acquisition anonymity). Keeps the system LIVE in sparse crowds where you can't earn receipts - this directly patches WRR's and QWT's sparse-crowd liveness gap.
(2) RECIPROCITY PATH (WRR, cheaper, incentive-aligned): relaying others' NOVEL blobs to DIVERSE neighbors earns DAcks (BLAKE2s MAC over H(B)||session_nonce||epoch_salt, keyed by the BLE-ECDH per-contact secret). Per local sliding epoch, credit = concave(min(novelty_served, distinct_PHY_radios)) - the diversity gate starves cliques. Earned credit lets you mint a token by paying a STEEPLY REDUCED PoSW difficulty N_relay << N_sweat (e.g. N_relay = N_sweat / 2^c, scaled by how much diverse novelty you served). CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (closes WRR's rebate loophole and the mint-by-relaying farm): the reciprocity discount must keep effective token cost STRICTLY ABOVE the value of the relay slot it buys, and N_relay is floored at a minimum >0 so even a node that forges diversity still pays sequential wall-clock per token. So honest relayers are token-rich (good for commons), leeches must burn full PoSW, and farms get neither a free pairing-mint nor a free relay-rebate.

SPEND (in the BLE handshake, never in the blob): a Fiat-Shamir NIZK proving 'I know (s,r,phi,Pi) s.t. com=Commit(s;r) AND PoSW.Verify(H(com),phi,Pi)=accept,' revealing exactly one derived nullifier nf = H('nf' || s || epoch_anchor), where epoch_anchor = commit-reveal of BOTH phones' 128b session nonces + both ephemeral pubkeys (defeats nullifier cross-neighbor replay). Neighbor checks the NIZK (hash-relation, cheap with STARK-friendly hash) + that nf is unused this session + grants one relay slot. Old tokens stay spendable (compute never wasted) but each yields a FRESH unpredictable nullifier per session - this is Hourglass's no-clock precompute defense, kept verbatim.

WHY THE BLEND BEATS EITHER ALONE: Hourglass alone has the worst R7 (compute decoupled from relaying) and the spread-farm risk reduces purely to 'rent sequential cores.' WRR alone has a weak R1 floor and a detection-based farm story foolable by spread farms. Stacked: a spread-out farm at crowd density now pays BOTH (a) sequential PoSW per token per device (Hourglass caps per-device rate, makes fake-novelty injection expensive) AND (b) must serve genuinely diverse real strangers to get the cheap path (WRR diversity gate). To mint cheaply AND at scale it must be both compute-heavy AND a genuinely good epidemic spreader - at which point it is improving the mesh (R7 turns the attack into a contribution). The honorable O(crowd-size)-hardware bound remains, stated plainly.

UNIFORMITY/INVARIANTS: tokens consumed in handshake only; spend bytes are fixed-length pseudorandom (STARK transcript + 256b nullifier) so real/cover/relayed spends are byte-indistinguishable; cover traffic runs the identical relay/DAck/mint/spend loop; mandatory fixed-R DAck padding + mandatory cover-spends kill the relaying-intensity and spend-rate side channels. No issuer, no internet, no global clock/tree/convergence: double-spend is local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set; epochs are each phone's own sliding monotonic window; self-destruct via signed absolute TTL on blobs + epoch_anchor freshness. Claims PRESERVES-ALL seven invariants (inherits WRR's clean status; the PoSW primitive swap adds no wire metadata).

BUILD: PoSW mint = BLAKE3/Poseidon DAG labeling + Merkle (background, any phone). DAck/diversity/cuckoo all reuse spec §6/§8 infrastructure. The one genuinely hard, novel piece is the ZK spend wrapper (proving PoSW-accept in ZK) - inherited from Hourglass, the largest software-correctness risk; mitigate with a STARK-friendly hash inside the PoSW so the proof is ~tens of KB / ~10-100ms verify.
recommended.whyItWins: It is the only construction that simultaneously (1) preserves all seven invariants (WRR spine), (2) has the best incentive-compatibility - the cheap mint path IS prosocial diverse relaying, attacking the tragedy-of-the-commons (WRR R7), (3) closes WRR's two real weaknesses: its vague VDF floor (replaced by buildable hash-only sequential PoSW with a true per-device physics cap, Hourglass R1) and its sparse-crowd liveness gap (the always-available SWEAT path keeps the system live with zero peers, Hourglass R4), and (4) keeps Hourglass's best properties - zero-wire-emission acquisition anonymity and no-beacon precompute defense via the per-session handshake nullifier anchor. The strict 'discount < slot value, N_relay floored >0' rule is what makes the blend safe: it fuses the two mint paths without reopening either scheme's loophole (Hourglass's mint-by-relaying rebate risk and WRR's free-clique-credit risk cancel because every token, however earned, still costs sequential wall-clock).
recommended.residualRisks: 1) BOTNET/CLOUD SWEAT-MINTING (inherited high from Hourglass): sequentiality caps per-device but not device-COUNT; a funded adversary renting many sequential cores mints many SWEAT tokens. Only economic mitigation (raise N), trading honest-user battery. The reciprocity path does NOT help here (cloud has no real crowd to serve), but it does mean cloud tokens cost full N_sweat each - the dominant attack against a funded adversary, unresolved. 2) ZK-WRAPPER SOUNDNESS (high): the whole unlinkability + no-free-forge story depends on a correct, sound NIZK proving PoSW-accept over an opening of com, with a non-malleable, properly domain-separated nullifier; a bug forges/re-spends tokens with no compute. Largest correctness risk, least off-the-shelf code. 3) SPREAD-OUT FARM AT CROWD DENSITY (high, shared by all diversity schemes): K real radios at K real locations each serving distinct strangers is indistinguishable from K honest heavy users and SHOULD mint - the honorable O(crowd) bound; partially defanged because such a farm improves the mesh, and now also pays N_relay per token. 4) Heterogeneous-hardware N-calibration: budget vs flagship phone get different mint rates with no global difficulty oracle (forbidden); accept a constant-factor advantage to fastest serial hardware. 5) Pagnia-Gaertner: DAck fair-exchange impossible without TTP; bounded to one-blob theft by gradual interleaved swap, not closeable. 6) Discount-tuning fragility: if N_relay is mis-set too low it reopens the relay-rebate farm; the 'strictly below slot value, floored >0' invariant must be conservatively parameterized and is the main operational knob.
recommended.openQuestions: 1) Exact discount curve: what function maps (diverse novelty served) -> N_relay such that effective cost stays provably above slot value across the full density range, given no global price oracle? Needs the §9 density-adaptive auto-knob to be specified concretely and shown not to be locally manipulable. 2) Can the ZK-spend wrapper be made cheap enough on low-end Android (the §5 puncturable-encryption-class benchmark question)? Pick Poseidon vs Rescue vs a sigma/Bulletproofs composition and benchmark verify-time + proof-size against BLE MTU/fragmentation and battery. 3) How big must N_sweat be to make cloud-farm minting uneconomic at stadium scale (50k phones) without making honest background mint a battery problem? This is the core unresolved R1/R7 tension. 4) Diversity-sketch parameters (window, K_div-equivalent cap, decay, HLL/cuckoo sizing) and their interaction with the PHY-observed-distinct-radio binding that prevents forged-session-key poisoning. 5) Does folding two mint paths into one token format create any linkage between a SWEAT-minted and a RELAY-minted token at spend time (must be byte-indistinguishable - verify the nullifier and transcript reveal nothing about which path minted)? 6) Self-destruct/crypto-shred interaction with long-lived re-spendable tokens: an absolute signed TTL on tokens (not just blobs) may be needed so stockpiles age out.

selection.combinationNote: The recommendation deliberately BLENDS the top two candidates rather than picking one, because their strengths and weaknesses are near-perfectly complementary. WRR (Witnessed Relay Reciprocity) is the spine: it is the ONLY preserves-all candidate, has the best R7 (the token source is prosocial diverse relaying, attacking the relay tragedy-of-the-commons the spec worries about), the lowest build cost (reuses §6/§8/§9 infrastructure the spec already mandates), and the cleanest R5 serverless/offline fit. But WRR has two real holes: (a) its per-device Sybil floor (R1) rests on a loosely-specified, phone-awkward class-group VDF, and (b) it has a sparse-crowd liveness gap (no peers => no receipts => can't mint). Hourglass (self-anchored PoSW + ZK nullifier) fixes both: its hash-based sequential PoSW is buildable on phone hash hardware with no trusted setup and gives a TRUE un-parallelizable per-device cap (the cleanest R1 physics), and its always-available pure-compute mint keeps the system live with zero peers. Hourglass's own weaknesses - worst R7 (compute decoupled from relaying) and a spread-farm that reduces to 'rent sequential cores' - are exactly what WRR's reciprocity path and diversity gate repair. The fusion is made SAFE by one invariant: every token, whether earned by sweat or by reciprocity, still costs sequential wall-clock (N_relay floored >0, discount strictly below the slot value it buys). That single rule cancels both schemes' loopholes - Hourglass's mint-by-relaying rebate risk and WRR's free-clique-credit risk - because a farm can no longer get a free pairing-mint OR a free relay-rebate; it must be both compute-heavy AND a genuinely good diverse spreader, at which point it is contributing to the mesh. Proof-of-Encounter and QWT contributed the diversity-gating and decorrelation insights already present in WRR; VOUCH and WITNESSED SWEAT were rejected as bases (critical shared-key SPOF and a knife-edge shared-MAC-key security argument respectively), though WITNESSED SWEAT's 'product of two scarcities' intuition is precisely what the blend operationalizes - here the two scarcities are sequential compute and diverse witnessed relay rather than compute and RF co-presence, avoiding the brittle ambient-RF extraction dependency.

redteam.primitive: LINK-LOCAL-TOKENS — Primitive A (link-local relay tokens) with the "Reciprocity Hourglass" token source (WRR spine: diversity-gated witnessed-relay credit + Hourglass hash-PoSW + ZK-nullifier; SWEAT and RECIPROCITY mint paths into one token format; spend = Fiat-Shamir NIZK revealing nf=H('nf'||s||epoch_anchor); double-spend = local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set).

attacks.title: Per-neighbor nullifier anchor makes one token worth N relay slots — the metering scheme's own replay defense defeats double-spend detection
attacks.mechanism: The spend nullifier is nf = H('nf' || s || epoch_anchor) where epoch_anchor = commit-reveal of BOTH phones' session nonces + both ephemeral pubkeys. The stated GOAL of that binding is to 'defeat nullifier cross-neighbor replay.' But it does the opposite for metering: by construction the SAME token (same s) yields a DIFFERENT, fresh, valid nullifier against every distinct neighbor. The double-spend store is explicitly 'local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set' — there is NO global view (invariant 6/spec). Therefore a single minted token is spendable exactly once PER NEIGHBOR PER SESSION, i.e. one token buys ~D relay slots where D = number of distinct neighbors you meet. In a dense gathering (invariant 7) D is in the hundreds to thousands. The literature is unambiguous: offline systems without a global view can only DETECT double-spend retrospectively and only by de-anonymizing the spender (Chaum/Brands result; SoK on offline CBDC). Here detection is impossible because anonymity-via-uniformity (invariant 3/4) means there is no payer identity to expose. So the rate-limit is not 'one relay per token' but 'one relay per token per neighbor' — which for an attacker meeting many neighbors is barely a limit at all. A farm node mints (or sweat-mints) ONE token and relays unlimited times across the venue, paying sequential cost once. This collapses the entire purpose of primitive A.
attacks.breaks: Defeats the rate-limit purpose of primitive A; lets a resourced attacker flood (the OPEN PROBLEM A is meant to solve). Does not break invariants 1-7 directly, but nullifies the metering the whole primitive exists for.

attacks.title: Cloud/botnet SWEAT-minting floods at O(crowd) cost the attacker controls, not O(crowd) the venue controls
attacks.mechanism: Conceded as residual risk #1 but understated. PoSW caps per-DEVICE mint rate, never device COUNT. N_sweat must be calibrated so honest phones can mint a few tokens/hour without draining battery — call it minutes-to-an-hour per token (the spec's own range). A rented fleet of sequential cores (or just GPUs running many independent sequential chains — sequentiality stops intra-token parallelism, NOT across-token parallelism) mints tokens embarrassingly in parallel: 10,000 cloud cores each producing 1 token/10min = 60k tokens/hour, all byte-indistinguishable from honest spends. Combined with attack #1 (each token = D slots), the effective flood budget is 60k * D ~= tens of millions of relay slots/hour. The cost asymmetry is backwards: the honest phone pays full N_sweat on a battery; the attacker pays a few cents/core-hour with no battery constraint and no real crowd needed. The 'attack becomes a contribution' framing (R7) FAILS for cloud tokens because cloud nodes have no real crowd to serve — they inject pure flood, not epidemic-useful relay. No-global-difficulty-oracle (invariant 6) means you cannot raise N adaptively in response to the flood, so the only lever (static high N) is paid by honest users' batteries.
attacks.breaks: Resourced attacker still floods; primitive A fails its design goal. Stresses invariant 7's 'venue-scale' assumption (attacker hardware bound is O(rented cores), not O(crowd)).

attacks.title: BLE PHY fingerprinting collapses the K-anonymity the token system depends on AND turns acquisition-anonymity into a fiction
attacks.mechanism: The design concedes device-linkage ~= 1.0 from PHY fingerprinting (and primitive D builds on this). But the token source claims 'best acquisition anonymity' for the SWEAT path ('emits nothing on the wire') and 'byte-indistinguishable' spends. Both claims are undercut by the conceded fingerprint. (a) SPEND linkability: every token spend is in a BLE handshake. With RF/PHY fingerprinting at ~99.8% device-ID accuracy (Tang et al.; UCSD/WCSNG; thn 2022) plus RSSI-based address-derandomization, a passive multi-receiver adversary in the venue links ALL of one device's spends to one physical radio regardless of byte-uniformity. The nullifier being fresh-per-session is irrelevant: the PHY ties them together. So 'real msg indistinguishable from cover and relayed' (invariant 3) holds at the BYTE layer but FAILS at the PHY layer — and the wire is the PHY. (b) The diversity gate (RECIPROCITY path) requires binding credit to 'distinct PHY radios' — meaning the protocol ITSELF observes and stores PHY identities to function. An honest node's diversity sketch is a self-incriminating log of which physical radios it met, when. Subpoena/seizure of one phone yields a contact graph. (c) ACQUISITION: even SWEAT 'emits nothing,' but the FIRST spend of a sweat token is observable, and the gap between a device appearing in the venue and its first spend leaks whether it sweat-minted (silent gap) vs relay-minted (visible DAck exchanges) — a path-distinguishing side channel the design's own open-question #5 admits is unverified.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 3 (uniformity) and invariant 4 (sealed-sender / no identity on wire) — the PHY layer carries a stable identity the crypto cannot erase. Diversity-gate state also weakens invariant 4 at rest.

attacks.title: Spread-out farm at crowd density mints the cheap RECIPROCITY path legitimately, then floods — the diversity gate cannot distinguish it from honest heavy users
attacks.mechanism: Conceded as residual #3 ('honorable O(crowd) bound') but its interaction with attack #1 is not. K physical farm radios at K real locations, each serving genuinely distinct strangers, earn legitimate DAcks and the diversity-gated discount (concave(min(novelty_served, distinct_PHY_radios))). They mint at the cheap N_relay rate. The spec's safety rule is 'effective token cost STRICTLY ABOVE the value of the relay slot it buys.' But attack #1 shows the slot value is actually D slots per token (one per neighbor), so the TRUE value of a token is ~D x (single-slot value), while the cost floor was set against a SINGLE slot's value. The 'strictly above slot value' invariant is therefore mis-anchored by a factor of D: the discount is provably-safe against the wrong denominator. A farm that serves real novelty cheaply mints tokens each worth D slots — it is paid to flood. Worse, the farm can be a 'spread amplifier': relay just enough genuine novelty to clear the diversity gate, then spend the resulting cheap tokens to inject its OWN flood blobs (or replay stale ones up to TTL). The mesh-improvement R7 framing only covers the relaying it does to EARN tokens, not the flood it does when SPENDING them.
attacks.breaks: Metering goal of primitive A; the discount-safety invariant ('cost > slot value') is violated once slot value is correctly counted as per-neighbor.

attacks.title: ZK-spend wrapper soundness is a single point of total failure with no fallback and the least off-the-shelf code
attacks.mechanism: Conceded as residual #2, correctly rated. Amplifying: the ENTIRE no-free-forge property of BOTH mint paths, AND unlinkability, AND the per-session nullifier freshness, all reduce to one bespoke NIZK proving 'PoSW.Verify(H(com),phi,Pi)=accept over an opening of com, revealing nf.' This is the novel, hard piece (spec admits 'largest software-correctness risk, least off-the-shelf code'). Failure modes: (a) a soundness bug forges tokens with ZERO compute, instantly collapsing the rate-limit globally and silently (no wire signal that a forged vs honest token was spent — uniformity hides the attack). (b) a malleability bug on the nullifier lets one token re-spend within the SAME neighbor/session (re-mauling nf), removing even the local rate-limit. (c) Fiat-Shamir transcript bugs (weak/under-bound challenge derivation — a recurring real-world class, e.g. Frozen Heart) break soundness across an entire implementation. There is no graceful degradation: unlike a leaf-level flood that uniformity tolerates, a broken token primitive means the venue has NO rate-limit and no way to detect it. Verify must also be cheap on low-end Android (open-question #2 unresolved) — pressure to use a faster, less-reviewed proof system raises bug probability.
attacks.breaks: If realized: total failure of primitive A and of acquisition/spend unlinkability (invariants 3/4). Until benchmarked it also threatens invariant 1 (full function offline) via verify-cost battery/latency on weak hardware.

attacks.title: Partition / sliding-epoch divergence enables token reuse and stale-blob replay across a blackout boundary
attacks.mechanism: Epochs are 'each phone's own sliding monotonic window' with no global clock (invariants 1/6). The nullifier is anchored to a per-session epoch_anchor (both nonces + ephemeral keys), and double-spend memory is per-neighbor-per-session. Two consequences in a partition: (a) After a network split (two ends of a stadium, or a blackout-neighborhood fragmenting), a node carries the SAME token into both partitions and spends it against the same neighbor in two DIFFERENT sessions — because the cuckoo set is per-SESSION, re-meeting the same neighbor in a fresh session resets the nullifier (fresh epoch_anchor => fresh nf). So even the single intended per-neighbor limit is only per-session, and sessions can be re-established repeatedly (drop and re-handshake) to mint fresh nullifiers from one token. This is a churn-driven double-spend with no forgery. (b) VDF/PoSW age-floor (primitive C) and signed TTLs depend on wall-clock; the PoSW iteration-count age-floor and the token 're-spendable forever' rule (open-question #6) mean stockpiled tokens minted before a blackout stay valid through it, so an attacker pre-mints a war-chest cheaply over days and dumps it during the high-value blackout window when honest mint rates are lowest.
attacks.breaks: Metering goal under churn/partition; bends the spirit of invariant 5 (self-destruct) for tokens, which have no TTL in the base design (open-question #6 admits this gap).

attacks.title: Loopix-style statistical disclosure / intersection on the token-acquisition and self-loop loops de-anonymizes originators
attacks.mechanism: The token system runs alongside primitive D (Poisson outbound + self-loops). Self-loops are 'sealed to your own key' and 'double as cover + active-attack detection + reachability sensor.' Loopix literature shows loops + Poisson delays resist a GLOBAL passive adversary statistically but remain vulnerable to long-run intersection / statistical-disclosure attacks, and self-loops specifically can be a fingerprint: a self-loop must eventually return to the SAME physical radio (PHY-linkable, attack #3). A multi-receiver adversary correlating (i) which radio emits a self-loop and (ii) which radio later 'receives' it confirms self-loops and can SUBTRACT them from the cover-traffic estimate, sharpening the intersection attack on real originations. Separately, the receive-before-originate gate (relay >=k novel IDs before originating) creates a timing signature: the adversary watches a radio's transition from pure-relay to first-origination; across multiple venues/sessions the set of radios present at each of a target message's plausible origination windows intersects down (classic disclosure). Token spends add a second observable stream to intersect against. Mark uncertainty: exact de-anon rate depends on crowd size, churn, and adversary receiver density — but the design's K-anonymity claim assumes these loops are pure noise, which the Loopix analyses show they are not under sustained observation.
attacks.breaks: Erodes invariant 3 (uniformity/indistinguishability) and the K-anonymity property of primitive D under a sustained multi-receiver adversary; severity bounded by crowd churn.

attacks.title: Diversity-sketch poisoning and DAck farming via colluding/forged sessions feeds the cheap mint path
attacks.mechanism: Credit = concave(min(novelty_served, distinct_PHY_radios)), with DAcks = BLAKE2s MAC over H(B)||session_nonce||epoch_salt keyed by BLE-ECDH per-contact secret. Two abuses: (a) Wash-trading novelty: a small clique pre-seeds each other with 'novel' blobs (that only THEY consider novel — novelty is local, no global registry) and issues mutual DAcks. The diversity gate's defense is the distinct_PHY_radios cap — but a clique with K real radios legitimately presents K distinct PHYs (attack #5), and the concave cap only limits magnitude, not the fact that a clique manufactures its own novelty stream to relay. (b) Pagnia-Gaertner (residual #5): DAck fair-exchange is impossible without a TTP, so a node can receive a relay, withhold its DAck, and re-extract value — bounded to one-blob theft per the spec, but at scale this is a steady credit leak. (c) The diversity sketch (HLL/cuckoo) parameters are unspecified (open-question #4); HLL is approximate and manipulable by an adversary who crafts inputs to inflate the distinct-count estimate, directly inflating the discount. The mitigation 'PHY-observed-distinct-radio binding' re-imports the privacy cost of attack #3.
attacks.breaks: Free-riding / cheap-mint inflation undermines the incentive-compatibility claim and the metering goal; the PHY-binding mitigation trades against invariant 4.

fixes.title: Re-anchor the nullifier to the TOKEN, not the neighbor: per-token global-within-session nullifier with gossiped local revocation
fixes.addresses: Attack #1 (one-token-per-neighbor) and #6a (per-session reset); partially #2/#4 (caps token reuse to 1 slot total).
fixes.howItWorks: Split the anchor. Keep a session-binding field to stop cross-neighbor REPLAY of a transcript, but derive the spent-marker from the token secret ALONE: nf_token = H('nf' || s) (one per token, forever), and additionally reveal a session-bound proof tag that ties THIS transcript to THIS handshake (so a recorded transcript can't be replayed verbatim to a third party). Each node gossips its locally-seen nf_token set epidemically in the SAME uniform flood (piggybacked, fixed-length, indistinguishable from blobs). A neighbor accepts a token only if nf_token is novel in its (now venue-shared, eventually-consistent) seen-set. This converts 'one slot per neighbor' into 'one slot per token venue-wide, modulo gossip propagation delay.' The residual double-spend window shrinks from D (all neighbors) to (neighbors reachable within one gossip-propagation epoch) — a large, quantifiable reduction. Crucially the gossiped nf set carries NO routing/targeting metadata (it's a flat set of opaque hashes), so invariant 2 holds; it's byte-uniform, so invariant 3 holds.

fixes.title: Bound token lifetime with a signed absolute TTL + PoSW age-floor so stockpiles and partition war-chests expire
fixes.addresses: Attack #6b (pre-mint war-chest dumped during blackout) and #2 (limits cloud stockpiling); closes open-question #6.
fixes.howItWorks: Attach to every token the same self-destruct machinery blobs already have (invariant 5): an absolute signed TTL inside the committed core, plus reuse primitive C's VDF/PoSW iteration count as a trustless wall-clock age-FLOOR AND age-CEILING. The PoSW already proves N sequential steps occurred (a lower bound on age); pin the TTL relative to the token's own epoch_anchor so a token is spendable only within [mint_time, mint_time + <=7d] consistent with the existing epoch-bucketing (<=7d). This makes cloud pre-minting a depreciating asset: tokens minted days before a blackout are dead by the time the blackout arrives, forcing the attacker to mint DURING the venue window at venue-realistic rates. Honest users are unaffected (they mint and spend within hours). No clock oracle needed — TTL is verified against the same per-phone sliding monotonic window already used for blobs, and the PoSW step-count gives a clock-skew-resistant floor.

fixes.title: Price the discount against the CORRECT denominator: cost floor must exceed (expected reachable-neighbors-per-token) x single-slot-value, not single-slot-value
fixes.addresses: Attack #4 (mis-anchored discount-safety invariant) and #1 (token worth D slots).
fixes.howItWorks: The spec's safety rule 'effective token cost STRICTLY ABOVE the slot value it buys' is correct in form but mis-instantiated. Once fix #1 reduces a token to ~1 venue-wide slot (or a small bounded number r within one gossip window), re-derive N_relay and N_sweat so cost > r x slot_value with r measured, not assumed =1. Until fix #1 lands, set the floor against an upper bound on D (max distinct neighbors per session, observable locally from the diversity sketch). This is a parameterization change, not new mechanism, so it preserves all invariants; it directly defangs the 'paid to flood' result by making each token genuinely cost more than the flooding it can buy. Pair with the density-adaptive auto-knob (open-question #1): clamp the discount using the LOCAL distinct-PHY estimate as the only input (no global oracle), accepting that a spread farm at honest density gets honest pricing (the conceded honorable O(crowd) bound).

fixes.title: Treat PHY-linkage as in-scope, not assumed-1.0-and-ignored: PHY-randomization hygiene + decouple metering-PHY-binding from stored identity
fixes.addresses: Attack #3 (spend/diversity PHY de-anon) and #8 (sketch poisoning via PHY).
fixes.howItWorks: The design concedes device-linkage ~= 1.0 then builds K-anonymity (primitive D) and the diversity gate on top of it — contradictory. Two concrete moves that preserve invariants: (a) Mandate aggressive BLE address + advertising-interval randomization and, where the radio permits, TX-power/timing jitter to RAISE the cost of RF fingerprinting from trivial to SDR-and-ML (it cannot reach 0, but the design currently does nothing). State plainly in the threat model that against an SDR+ML adversary anonymity degrades to PSEUDONYMITY-per-venue, and scope claims accordingly (honest correction of invariant 3/4 claims rather than a silent overclaim). (b) For the diversity gate, bind credit to a PER-SESSION ephemeral PHY observation that is consumed into the concave counter and then DISCARDED — never persisted as a contact log. Store only the running concave credit scalar + a Bloom/cuckoo of nullifiers, not the radio identities. This keeps the starve-the-clique property while removing the at-rest contact graph (limits attack #3b on seizure).

fixes.title: Harden and de-risk the ZK-spend wrapper: use battle-tested transcript hygiene, add a non-ZK fallback rate-limit, and fail closed
fixes.addresses: Attack #5 (ZK soundness single point of failure) and forging/replay collapse.
fixes.howItWorks: (a) Make the Fiat-Shamir challenge bind the ENTIRE statement including the full PoSW relation, com, epoch_anchor, and domain separators (the Frozen Heart class of bugs comes from under-binding the transcript) — and specify this in a test vector suite with negative tests (malformed openings, re-mauled nullifiers, replayed transcripts must all reject). (b) Use a mature STARK-friendly stack with public reference implementations rather than a hand-rolled sigma composition for the PoSW-accept relation; accept larger proofs for fewer bugs. (c) Add DEFENSE IN DEPTH so a wrapper bug is not instantly total: keep a cheap secondary local rate-limit independent of the ZK — e.g. cap relay slots granted to any single PHY-session to a small constant regardless of how many valid tokens it presents, so even unlimited forged tokens can't exceed a per-neighbor relay quota. This bounds a soundness break to a leaf-level flood that uniformity already tolerates, instead of a venue-wide rate-limit collapse. (d) Benchmark verify on low-end Android before committing (open-question #2); fail CLOSED (reject token, fall back to the per-PHY quota) if verify exceeds a latency budget.

fixes.title: Subtract self-loops from the cover estimate and rate-limit re-handshakes to blunt intersection / churn attacks
fixes.addresses: Attack #7 (Loopix intersection/self-loop fingerprint) and #6a (re-handshake nullifier reset).
fixes.howItWorks: (a) Because self-loops return to the same PHY (attack #3) and a sustained adversary can identify and subtract them (Loopix statistical-disclosure literature), do NOT count self-loops toward the anonymity-set claim; treat them only as the active-attack/reachability sensor they also are, and size REAL cover traffic (relayed + decoy) independently so the K-anonymity argument doesn't rest on subtractable noise. (b) To stop churn-driven nullifier resets (#6a), make the per-neighbor double-spend memory survive re-handshakes within an epoch: key the cuckoo set on the LONG-LIVED per-contact ECDH secret (already derived) rather than the per-session nonce, so re-meeting the same neighbor in a new session does NOT reset the nf check. This costs a small amount of per-contact state but closes the drop-and-re-handshake mint amplifier without any global view. Both changes are local, carry no wire metadata (invariant 2), and keep spends byte-uniform (invariant 3).

redteam.primitive: CRYPTO-SHRED — crypto-shred-on-read via puncturable encryption (polleneus v0.3 primitive B): one long-lived pubkey per identity, epoch-bucketed (<=7d), reading punctures the key so ciphertext is "never decryptable again" (FS by default, serverless), plus a decoupled delete-token = H(domain_delete||secret) that purges cooperating buffers without proving the blob was real and can mint decoy deletes.

attacks.title: Puncture-state growth is unaffordable on weak phones at gathering scale (the core feasibility wall)
attacks.mechanism: Puncturable encryption with perfect/negligible correctness error provably requires a secret key that grows at least linearly in the number of punctures, because the key must encode the list of punctured tags (Derler et al., Bloom Filter Encryption, EUROCRYPT'18 / J.Cryptol'21). The only known way to bound that growth is the Bloom-Filter-Encryption (BFE) relaxation, but BFE's secret key is HUGE up front: at a 120-bit security level on BLS12-381 the BFE/BFKEM secret key is ~700 MB (SafetyPin, OSDI'20 reports BFE keys '>64 MB ... over 700 MB'), shrinking only as you puncture. In the soup, every trial-decrypt success against a real message is a puncture event, and self-loops (primitive D) are sealed to your OWN key — each returning self-loop you read also punctures. A phone that relays/reads thousands of IDs across a 7-day epoch faces either (a) a multi-hundred-MB at-rest key blob per active epoch on a 2-3 GB-budget device that is already holding 1-2M blobs (Sec 11), or (b) non-BFE PE where decryption time is LINEAR in puncture count and a single puncture costs 30s-minutes (Green-Miers S&P'15 era). The spec's own line 'benchmark on weak phones (open item)' is doing all the load-bearing work and the literature answer is: it does not fit. Epoch-bucketing to <=7d does not save you because within ONE epoch the puncture count at stadium density is already in the thousands.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 1 (full function in a blackout — a phone that cannot allocate the key or stalls minutes per read is non-functional) and Invariant 7 (dense-gathering scope is exactly where puncture count explodes). Also violates the implicit weak-phone deployability the whole project rests on.

attacks.title: BFE's non-negligible false-positive error silently breaks both 'never decryptable again' AND trial-decrypt
attacks.mechanism: BFE buys its cheap, delete-only puncture by tolerating a NON-NEGLIGIBLE correctness error inherited from the Bloom filter's false-positive rate. Two failure directions, both fatal to the spec's claims. (1) FALSE-POSITIVE RETENTION: with probability ~p (the BF FP rate, deliberately non-negligible to keep the key small) a ciphertext you THINK you punctured remains decryptable, because puncture only deletes the key shares for the indices a tag hashes to and a colliding later tag can leave a usable path. So 'reading punctures the key so that ciphertext can NEVER be decrypted again' (line 113) is false: it becomes 'cannot be decrypted again EXCEPT with probability p' — a non-zero seizure-recovery and re-read oracle. (2) FALSE-NEGATIVE on FRESH mail: a brand-new legitimate message can hash into already-punctured BF indices and fail to decrypt even though the recipient never read it — a self-inflicted message loss whose rate climbs as the epoch fills. In the async soup this is indistinguishable from 'not mine,' so the recipient carries and re-shares their own undelivered message until TTL death, learning nothing. The spec leans on key-committing AEAD to make trial-decrypt 'unambiguous' (line 116), but key-committing AEAD only disambiguates which KEY succeeded; it cannot resurrect a BFE decapsulation that the FP/FN structure has already corrupted.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 5 (self-destruct / crypto-shred is probabilistic, not absolute — contradicts 'never decryptable again') and Invariant 3 (anonymity via uniformity assumes reliable trial-decrypt to tell 'mine' from 'carry'; FN-on-fresh-mail makes your own real message behave like cover and never deliver).

attacks.title: Message-Suppression Attack defeats 'FS by default' against the exact adversary it is sold against (device seizure)
attacks.mechanism: Puncture-on-read only happens if the recipient actually receives and reads the ciphertext. A well-known PE limitation is the Message-Suppression Attack (MSA): an adversary who can keep a ciphertext from reaching the recipient ensures the key is NEVER punctured for it, so on later key leakage that ciphertext is decryptable. In polleneus this adversary is cheap and already in-model: the multi-sensor mesh / co-located adversary (threat model rows 4-5) can record blobs off the air and simply ensure (via eviction pressure, jamming a victim's link, or just being faster) that the victim's device drops or never reads a specific blob. Then they seize the device (row 8) and use the un-punctured long-lived key to decrypt the captured ciphertext. Because the key is LONG-LIVED across the whole epoch (line 113), every unread-but-captured blob in that epoch is recoverable. This is precisely the seizure threat the spec claims crypto-shred defends (line 147: 'a seized device cant recover read messages') — but the guarantee evaporates for any message the seizer prevented from being read, and the seizer controls that.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 5 (self-destruct/FS-by-default) against threat-model row 8 (device-seizure/coercion). The honest framing in Sec 7 only disclaims durable deletion against a hostile RETAINER; it does NOT disclaim that an unread message plus a seized key is plaintext, which is a stronger break than the doc admits.

attacks.title: Crypto-shred does not shred against seizure: flash wear-leveling / FTL / Factory Access retains the pre-puncture key
attacks.mechanism: Puncture is a logical key-update: write new (smaller) key, delete old shares. On phone flash this never reliably erases the OLD bytes. Wear-leveling + the flash translation layer remap logical sectors to new physical pages and leave the prior copy in retired/over-provisioned blocks that are never overwritten for the life of the device (EFF SSD; SSD-forensics literature); retired-near-failure blocks keep contents indefinitely; and Factory Access Mode / firmware low-level reads can pull raw NAND. Encrypt-at-rest (Sec 6) only helps while the phone is LOCKED — under coercion (row 8) or after unlock the at-rest key is available, and then the un-erased pre-puncture decryption-key shares for already-read messages are physically recoverable. So 'their copy is unreadable to themselves and any future seizer' (line 147) overstates: the ciphertext blob may be gone from the inbox, but if the corresponding pre-puncture key fragment lingers in NAND, the read message is recoverable from any retained ciphertext copy (and the adversary, row 4, retains ciphertext off the air).
attacks.breaks: Invariant 5 (crypto-shred). The shred is a software erase on a substrate that does not honor software erase; the guarantee is contingent on hardware secure-erase / inline hardware key-wrapping the spec never specifies.

attacks.title: Decoupled delete-token is an unauthenticated buffer-flush DoS
attacks.mechanism: delete-token = H(domain_delete||secret) (line 148) is decoupled from message-ID by design so it 'purges cooperating buffers without proving the blob was real.' But decoupling from message-ID means the receiving buffer has no way to bind a presented token to a specific blob it actually holds — purge-on-token is therefore a content-addressed delete keyed only by a hash the originator chose. There is no proof-of-work, no VDF, and no relay-token gate specified on delete-tokens (the VDF in primitive C is over the blob's immutable core, not over deletes). An attacker who can mint H(domain_delete||secret) for secrets they generate can broadcast a firehose of valid-looking delete-tokens; cooperating buffers must at minimum spend a lookup per token, and if the matching rule is loose (e.g., prefix/sketch-based to be 'decoupled'), an attacker can craft tokens that collide with or over-match honest blobs and evict real traffic. The 'mint decoy deletes for your own cover blobs' feature (line 148) is explicitly a token-flood generator turned into a sanctioned mode, so the system cannot rate-limit deletes without killing the decoy-cover story.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 2/3 indirectly (a cheap, identity-free wire signal that selectively removes blobs is a targeting primitive the moment match is not perfectly blob-bound) and the anti-abuse spine of Sec 9 (deletes bypass the VDF + link-token cost that gate every other wire action). Severity hinges on the unspecified match rule; if matching requires the FULL preimage of a held blob's secret it is mostly defanged — but then it is no longer decoupled and decoy-minting for cover blobs you did not author is impossible.

attacks.title: Delete-token decoy/real distinction and timing is a linkability / confirmation oracle
attacks.mechanism: The decoy-delete feature is justified as hiding a real delete among decoys, but in a pure-flooding mesh with device-linkage ~= 1.0 (Sec 10), the ADVERSARY observes which physical radio emits a delete-token and WHEN. A real delete is correlated with a real read/puncture event (you delete what you actually consumed); decoys are not. If decoy-delete emission is not driven by the SAME Poisson process and SAME rate as real deletes (primitive D mixes ORIGINATIONS, not deletes — deletes are not in its enumerated outbound queue {relayed, dummies, self-loops, real}), then delete-token timing leaks 'this device just consumed a real message,' which is exactly the read-receipt deanonymization Signal sealed-sender was designed to avoid (Sec 18 prior art). Even with perfect byte-uniformity of the token, the EVENT of emitting it from a PHY-fingerprinted handset is a side channel. Additionally, because the token is H(domain_delete||secret) and the SAME secret derives the message-ID = H(domain||secret||...), an adversary who ever learns a secret (seizure) can link a previously observed delete-token to its message-ID retroactively, re-proving the blob was real and undoing the 'without proving the original blob was real' property.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 3 (real indistinguishable from cover) and Invariant 4 (sealed-sender / no identity-correlated event on the wire), via timing + the shared-secret linkage between delete-token and message-ID.

attacks.title: Replay / re-injection in the async soup forces unbounded re-puncture and amplifies key-state DoS
attacks.mechanism: The soup is a store-and-forward flood with no global state; the seen-record (Sec 6) is per-device and sliding-window. An adversary (or just the mesh's natural churn) can re-inject a victim's already-read ciphertext from another carrier after it ages out of the victim's seen-window but before TTL (<=7d). On re-receipt the victim either (a) re-reads -> attempts another puncture for an already-punctured tag (wasted linear-cost work, and in BFE possibly a no-op that still costs a scan), or (b) the key-committing AEAD trial-decrypt now must run against a key whose puncture-set is large, paying the linear decryption cost the literature warns about every single re-injection. An attacker who farms many distinct novel-looking blobs at one victim (each a forced puncture) drives the victim's puncture count and per-read cost up deliberately — a targeted battery/compute exhaustion that needs no identity and rides the legitimate relay path. The relay-token gate (token source A) limits relaying-OUT per neighbor, but does not limit how many distinct blobs a victim must trial-decrypt and (on success) puncture INBOUND.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 1 (a phone driven to multi-second reads and runaway key state loses function in the blackout) and Invariant 7 (the attack is cheapest exactly at dense-gathering scale). This is the puncture-state DoS the literature flags, instantiated by the soup's lack of global dedup.

attacks.title: 'FS by default, serverless' is only as strong as the puncture's atomicity and the unread-message window
attacks.mechanism: Genuine forward secrecy requires that at any instant the key cannot decrypt PAST consumed messages. With puncture-on-read, the FS guarantee is fine-grained but has two leaks the 'by default' phrasing hides. (1) PRE-READ WINDOW: every message sits in the inbox/soup decryptable until the user opens it; a seizure during that window (the common case — people do not read instantly in a chaotic gathering) recovers everything unread, which at protest/blackout scale can be the majority of inbound. This is weaker than ratchet-based FS (Signal), where keys advance on RECEIPT/clock, not on user READ. (2) NON-ATOMIC PUNCTURE: if the device crashes / is grabbed between 'decrypt-and-show' and 'persist the punctured key,' the old key survives and the message is recoverable — and on a phone, fsync of a hundred-MB BFE key is not instant, widening this window. So serverless FS here is really 'FS conditioned on (you read it) AND (the puncture durably committed) AND (no FP retention) AND (NAND actually erased)' — a conjunction of four conditions the spec treats as one default.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 5 (self-destruct / FS-by-default is conditional, not default). Does not break it categorically, but the marketing-grade 'FS by default' claim (lines 99, 113) is not supported without specifying read-latency policy, atomic key-commit, and the BFE error budget.

fixes.title: Replace puncture-on-READ with epoch-ratcheted forward-secure decryption (puncture-on-RECEIPT or on time)
fixes.addresses: MSA seizure break; pre-read-window FS leak; the worst of the puncture-state growth (decouples key size from per-message reads)
fixes.howItWorks: Drop the unbounded per-message puncture model in favor of a small fixed number of FINE-GRAINED TIME EPOCHS within the <=7d TTL (e.g., hourly sub-epochs via a forward-secure / hierarchical-IBE-style key evolution, as in the time-based BFE construction). The decryption key auto-ratchets forward on the gossip-median mesh clock (Sec 6) — already in the spec — so keys for elapsed sub-epochs are deleted whether or not the user read anything. This makes FS depend on TIME, not on the user reading, killing the Message-Suppression Attack (the seizer cannot stop the clock) and the pre-read window (unread mail in an elapsed sub-epoch is also gone). Per-message 'disappear-after-read' becomes an OPTIONAL extra puncture on top, not the FS mechanism. Key size is now bounded by (sub-epochs remaining), a small constant, not by puncture count. This is exactly the design that made BFE 0-RTT 'efficient enough to deploy' (Derler et al.).

fixes.title: Specify the construction as time-based BFKEM with a published, deployable parameter set and a hard puncture-count cap
fixes.addresses: Puncture-state growth/DoS; weak-phone performance; the unbounded-re-puncture replay DoS
fixes.howItWorks: Commit to a concrete BFKEM instance (e.g., pairing-based on BLS12-381 or a lattice/code BFKEM for PQ) with parameters chosen so the per-epoch secret key is bytes-to-low-MB, not hundreds of MB: set the Bloom filter (n = max expected punctures per sub-epoch, target FP error budget) explicitly, publish the resulting key size + per-puncture cost + per-decrypt cost on a low-end Android reference device (the spec's own open benchmark, made a release gate in P6). Enforce a HARD cap on punctures per sub-epoch; when hit, ratchet to the next sub-epoch (forced key roll) instead of growing the key — turning the replay/re-puncture exhaustion attack into a bounded, self-healing event rather than unbounded growth. Reuse the existing sliding-window seen-record (Sec 6) to suppress re-decrypt of already-seen IDs BEFORE attempting a puncture, so re-injection costs a cuckoo lookup, not a decapsulation.

fixes.title: Make the BFE correctness error explicit and asymmetric: tolerate false-negatives, never claim absolute 'never decryptable'
fixes.addresses: BFE false-positive retention (the 'never decryptable again' overclaim) and false-negative on fresh mail
fixes.howItWorks: Honestly restate the crypto-shred guarantee as probabilistic: 'after read, this ciphertext is decryptable with probability <= p' for a published p (e.g., 2^-20 to 2^-40), tuned by the BF parameters — and surface that in the truth-in-labeling UX (Sec 13), consistent with the project's honesty ethos. Defend against the false-NEGATIVE delivery loss by NOT punturing the index path on trial-decrypt FAILURE (only on success) and by sizing the BF so the cumulative FP/FN over a full sub-epoch stays under the delivery-loss budget; the forced sub-epoch ratchet (fix above) resets the filter occupancy so FN rate cannot creep toward 1. Pair with a per-message ephemeral-DH layer (sealed-sender already uses a fresh ephemeral per message, line 114) so that even an FP-retained BFE share alone does not yield plaintext without the message ephemeral — a belt-and-suspenders that makes the residual p漏 require BOTH the FP AND the retained ephemeral.

fixes.title: Two-layer key wrapping so 'shred' is a hardware-erasable single secret, not a software delete on NAND
fixes.addresses: Flash wear-leveling / FTL / Factory-Access seizure recovery of pre-puncture key bytes; non-atomic puncture commit
fixes.howItWorks: Never write the evolving decryption key to flash in the clear. Store the puncturable/forward-secure key state encrypted under a wrapping key held in hardware (StrongBox / Secure Element / TEE-backed Keystore with setUnlockedDeviceRequired + auth-bound, and where available hardware key-attestation). Puncture/ratchet then rewrites only the ENCRYPTED blob; the security of shred reduces to erasing/rotating the SE-resident wrapping key, which hardware can destroy atomically and which never hits wear-leveled NAND in plaintext. For panic/duress (Sec 6) the wipe becomes 'destroy the SE wrapping key' — instant, hardware-honored crypto-erase of the whole epoch state, immune to NAND remnants. This converts the unreliable software erase into the well-understood crypto-erase pattern (encrypt-then-destroy-the-key) the storage-security literature endorses. Caveat: depends on SE/TEE availability and integrity; surface in-app where unavailable (consistent with the iOS-background-style honest limits).

fixes.title: Gate and blob-bind the delete-token: VDF/relay-token-priced, prefix-free, perfectly matched, and Poisson-mixed
fixes.addresses: Delete-token flood DoS; loose-match eviction of honest blobs; delete-timing confirmation oracle; retroactive delete<->message-ID linkage
fixes.howItWorks: Three changes. (1) PRICE IT: require the same anti-abuse cost as any other wire action — a delete-token spend must carry a VDF proof (primitive C) and/or be consumed against a link-local relay token (token source A) per neighbor per session, so a delete firehose costs sequential wall-clock per token exactly like a flood. (2) BIND IT EXACTLY: a buffer purges a held blob ONLY on an exact, full-length match between the presented token and a per-blob delete-tag the originator embedded at mint (delete-tag = H(domain_delete||secret) committed INSIDE the sealed transcript, line 117), so a token can never over-match or evict a blob the attacker did not author. This keeps 'purge without proving the blob was real' to outside observers (the tag is opaque) while removing the loose-match eviction vector — you can still mint decoy deletes for your OWN cover blobs, just not for others'. (3) MIX IT: add deletes (real and decoy) to primitive D's Poisson outbound queue {relayed, dummies, self-loops, real, DELETES} so delete EMISSION timing is decorrelated from read events, closing the confirmation oracle; and accept that the seizure-time secret->token linkage is inherent (a secret reveals everything that secret made) and disclaim it honestly rather than claiming the token hides realness against a seizer.

fixes.title: Atomic commit + read-latency policy so FS is not silently conditional
fixes.addresses: Non-atomic puncture window; the 'FS by default' overclaim
fixes.howItWorks: Make the read->puncture->persist sequence atomic: decrypt to a transient buffer, write the punctured/ratcheted (wrapped) key state and fsync BEFORE rendering plaintext, journaling so a crash mid-puncture replays to the post-puncture state on restart (never the pre-puncture one). Combine with the time-ratchet (first fix) so even if a user never reads, FS still advances. Then state the guarantee precisely in-app: 'past READ or EXPIRED-sub-epoch messages are unrecoverable on this device once committed; unread messages in the current sub-epoch are recoverable until the sub-epoch ends.' This is the honest, defensible version of 'FS by default' and matches the project's truth-in-labeling soul-check.

redteam.primitive: VDF mint-cost + age-floor (polleneus v0.3 primitive C): each blob carries a class-group VDF proof over its immutable core; sequential evaluation so ASIC/GPU advantage allegedly collapses to a small constant; iteration count doubles as a trustless wall-clock age-floor to backstop immortal-message/clock-skew; verify cheap, generation off the send path.
