attacks.title: Age-floor is a compute LOWER bound, not a wall-clock UPPER bound — it cannot prevent backdating, immortal messages, or clock-skew (the exact thing it claims to fix)
attacks.mechanism: A VDF/PoSW iteration count N only proves that AT LEAST T seconds of sequential work were performed SOMETIME before verification. It says NOTHING about WHEN that work started relative to the blob's claimed birth, and nothing about an upper bound on the blob's age. The primitive claims the age-floor 'backstops immortal-message / clock-skew.' It does the opposite of an immortal-message backstop: (1) Backdating / freshness forgery — the immutable core that the VDF is computed over is attacker-chosen. To make a blob look 'computed N iterations ago,' the adversary simply runs the VDF over the desired core and the proof is valid the instant it finishes; nothing ties the VDF's start to any external time reference (Invariant 1 forbids a clock). So an attacker can mint a blob today whose VDF 'proves' it is old, or whose absolute signed TTL (Invariant 5) is interpreted against a self-asserted clock. (2) Immortal messages — the only true self-destruct mechanism here is the absolute signed TTL on the blob, not the VDF. The VDF age-FLOOR can only force a message to be at least T old; it can never force expiry. An attacker who wants an immortal message just keeps re-minting (cheap relative to a venue lifetime) or never includes a tight TTL. The VDF adds zero expiry power. The 'age-floor backstops immortal-message' claim is simply false: a floor is the wrong direction for killing immortality. Known: PoSW/VDF proves elapsed sequential effort but provides no trusted start-time anchor without an external beacon (which is forbidden by Invariant 1).
attacks.breaks: Invariant 5 (self-destruct / absolute signed TTL). The stated backstop function is unachievable; clock-skew and immortal-message resistance are not actually provided by the VDF.

attacks.title: Precompute / stockpile amortization defeats the per-blob mint-cost as an anti-spam economic gate
attacks.mechanism: Generation is explicitly 'off the send path' and the VDF is over an attacker-chosen immutable core. There is no binding between the VDF input and any unpredictable, per-venue, just-in-time challenge (there can't be — Invariant 1 forbids a global beacon and Invariant 2 forbids routing metadata). Therefore an adversary precomputes an arbitrarily large warehouse of valid (core, VDF-proof) blobs over weeks/months on rented sequential cores, then dumps them into the venue in a flood burst. The 'mint-cost' is paid once, offline, at leisure, and amortized across an entire campaign. This is the classic offline-precompute / time-memory-tradeoff failure of any puzzle whose challenge is not freshly and unpredictably bound to the moment of use. The cost-per-blob at flood time is effectively zero; the VDF gates nothing at the instant that matters. Note this is strictly worse than a hashcash-style scheme bound to a recipient nonce, because the soup is broadcast (no recipient to bind to without violating Invariants 2/4).
attacks.breaks: The anti-spam / Sybil-economic purpose of primitive C. Does not directly break an invariant, but the primitive fails its own goal because Invariants 1 and 2 forbid the freshness anchor that would make mint-cost matter.

attacks.title: Internal contradiction with the SELECTED token source: class-group VDF on phones is the exact thing the chosen design rejected — 'small constant' ASIC claim is false
attacks.mechanism: The selected token source (primitive A, the Reciprocity Hourglass) explicitly DELETES the class-group VDF and replaces it with hash-based sequential PoSW, giving reasons that are direct attacks on primitive C: 'phones have hash hardware, no fast class-group arithmetic, no trusted setup, no group of unknown order.' Primitive C as written re-introduces exactly that liability. Concretely: (a) ASIC advantage is NOT 'a small constant.' Measured class-group squaring ASIC speedups are ~14x vs optimized C++ on Apple M1 and ~31x for the modular-inverse subroutine (TCHES / VDF Alliance results). Repeated-squaring VDFs have a real, funded-adversary hardware gap; 'collapses to a small constant' understates it by an order of magnitude. (b) Group-size cost: published analysis shows ~830-bit class-group discriminants do NOT reach 128-bit security; ~3300 bits are needed. Big-integer class-group arithmetic at 3300 bits is brutal on a budget Android, while a flagship or rented ASIC sails through — heterogeneous-hardware advantage is large precisely where it hurts (weak-phone honest users). (c) Two incompatible primitive specs now coexist in v0.3 (class-group VDF in C vs hash PoSW in the chosen token A); whichever is built, the other's analysis is void.
attacks.breaks: Anonymity-via-uniformity (Invariant 3) indirectly: if weak phones can't mint in time they fall back to relay-only or skip cover, making real originators a distinguishable subpopulation. Primarily breaks design coherence and the stated 'small constant' security claim.

attacks.title: Weak-phone mint latency destroys the 1-minute 'message-in-a-bottle to the person next to me' use case
attacks.mechanism: A VDF/PoSW age-floor calibrated to be meaningful as wall-clock (minutes, per the Hourglass 'minutes-to-an-hour' calibration that primitive C shares in spirit) means the FIRST real message a user sends incurs full sequential mint latency on their own device, on the send path from the user's perspective even if 'off the send path' technically (the blob can't go out until minted). For a stadium/protest 'tell the person 20 meters away' interaction, a multi-minute mint is a UX dead end. Mitigations all fail under the invariants: pre-minting requires the VDF over the immutable CORE, but the core includes the message content, so you cannot pre-mint a blob whose content you don't yet know; pre-minting a content-independent 'shell' and binding content later either (a) breaks the 'VDF over immutable core' guarantee (content can be swapped post-mint → backdating attack above), or (b) requires a separate freshness binding that Invariants 1/2 forbid. On a budget phone with the 3300-bit arithmetic and ASIC-relative slowness, the latency is multiples of a flagship's. Result: the system is unusable for its primary dense-gathering scenario on the median attendee's phone.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 7 (dense-gathering scope / the actual stadium-protest use case). Also bends Invariant 3 (uniformity) because fast-phone vs slow-phone mint timing becomes an observable origination-timing distribution that correlates with device class.

attacks.title: Verify-cost asymmetry DoS — cheap-per-blob verify is still a Sybil-amplifiable flood, and class-group proof verification is not free on a phone
attacks.mechanism: Even granting 'verify cheap,' verification is not zero, and in a pure-flooding soup every receiver must verify every blob it sees, including all cover and all relayed traffic (Invariant 3 demands the verify path be identical for real/cover/relayed). An N-device farm (residual risk #1/#3 of the token source) at crowd density floods K x throughput of valid-but-junk blobs (each carrying a genuine, expensive-to-mint-but-cheap-to-verify proof from the precompute warehouse). The victim phones burn CPU/battery verifying the flood; this is a battery-exhaustion DoS, not a bandwidth DoS, and it is amplified by the broadcast topology. Worse: an attacker can also submit malformed proofs that are MAXIMALLY expensive to reject (e.g., proofs that pass cheap structural checks but fail only at the final, costliest class-group exponentiation/Fiat-Shamir step), turning 'cheap verify' into 'cheap for honest, expensive to reject for adversarial.' Pietrzak-style proofs have larger verification than Wesolowski; the wrong choice multiplies victim cost. Because there is no account/server (Invariant 6) and no targeting metadata (Invariant 2), there is no way to rate-limit a flooding peer except primitive A's relay tokens — which DON'T gate first-hop origination flooding into a victim's radio range.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 7 (the venue's devices, mostly battery-constrained phones, are the resource being exhausted). Bends Invariant 1's 'full function in a blackout' since blackout = no recharge, so battery DoS is more damaging.

attacks.title: N-device farm throughput: sequentiality caps per-device rate but not device count; broadcast flooding has no per-origination gate
attacks.mechanism: This is residual risk #1 of the selected token source, applied to primitive C. Sequentiality bounds ONE core to one blob per T seconds, but a funded adversary rents M sequential cores (cloud has plenty of fast serial CPUs; class-group squaring even runs faster there) and mints M/T blobs per second offline, forever, into the precompute warehouse. At flood time the farm broadcasts them via K cheap BLE radios placed around the venue. Primitive A's relay tokens are spent in the BLE handshake to rate-limit RELAYING per neighbor — they do NOT gate a node ORIGINATING fresh blobs into its own radio neighborhood (origination has no handshake counterparty to spend a token to). So the VDF mint-cost is the ONLY economic gate on origination flooding, and we just showed it is precompute-amortizable to ~0. Net: a modestly funded adversary saturates the uniform soup with valid blobs, drowning real messages and exhausting victim batteries, with the K-radio placement also defeating primitive D's first-sighting-graph K-anonymity (the farm controls first sightings).
attacks.breaks: Invariant 2 (pure flooding) is turned against the network — flooding with no targeting is exactly what makes origination-flooding ungated. Invariant 7 (venue saturated). Degrades Invariant 3 (real messages no longer buried in HONEST cover; they're buried in adversary-controlled cover, which an adversary who knows its own blobs can subtract out — uniformity holds for outsiders but the flooder gains a distinguisher over its own vs others' traffic).

attacks.title: No trusted setup is true for class groups, but discriminant/parameter selection is unspecified and silently security-critical (low-order-element trapdoor)
attacks.mechanism: Class groups of imaginary quadratic fields are indeed transparent (correctly avoiding RSA-modulus trusted setup), so the naive 'trusted-setup backdoor' attack does NOT apply — credit where due. BUT the security of Wesolowski/Pietrzak VDFs rests on the adaptive-root and low-order-element assumptions, which require the discriminant to be -p with p prime, p ≡ 3 (mod 4) (a fundamental discriminant), and large enough (≥ ~3300 bits for 128-bit, not the commonly-deployed ~830 bits). Primitive C specifies NONE of this. If the discriminant is hard-coded per-build and chosen poorly (non-fundamental, or with a known small class-group factor / low-order element), an attacker who knows or finds a low-order element can forge Pietrzak proofs (the low-order assumption is exactly what Pietrzak soundness needs). Since there is no server to rotate parameters and no account system (Invariants 1, 6), a bad baked-in discriminant is unfixable in-field and silently breaks every mint-cost guarantee. This is a 'looks transparent, is actually a single point of cryptographic failure' trap.
attacks.breaks: The soundness of the mint-cost itself (no invariant directly, but the primitive's security collapses). Interacts with Invariant 1/6 (no in-field parameter rotation) making it catastrophic if mis-set.

fixes.title: Drop the standalone class-group VDF; unify on the selected token source's hash-based sequential PoSW (Cohen-Pietrzak / Poseidon) and treat 'mint-cost' as that token, not a per-blob proof
fixes.addresses: Internal-contradiction attack; weak-phone latency; ASIC 'small constant' falsehood; discriminant/parameter trap; verify-cost DoS.
fixes.howItWorks: Primitive A already specifies a buildable hash-only sequential PoSW with a per-device physics cap, STARK-friendly verify (~tens of KB, ~10-100ms), no group of unknown order, no discriminant selection, and runs on phone hash hardware. Make the blob's anti-spam gate be 'this origination consumed one PoSW token' (verified locally on receipt via the same cheap NIZK wrapper) instead of an embedded class-group VDF. This eliminates the class-group arithmetic (no 3300-bit big-int math on budget phones), removes the unspecified discriminant single-point-of-failure, and collapses two contradictory specs into one. ASIC story becomes 'rent sequential hash cores' (honestly stated residual risk #1) rather than the larger 14-31x class-group ASIC gap. Verify is uniformly the cheap STARK check for real/cover/relayed (preserves Invariant 3).

fixes.title: Re-scope the 'age-floor' from a fake wall-clock to its only honest function: a clock-skew SANITY FLOOR that bounds minimum effort, and rely on the signed absolute TTL (Invariant 5) — not the VDF — for expiry/immortality
fixes.addresses: Critical age-floor attack (backdating, immortal-message, clock-skew backstop being the wrong direction).
fixes.howItWorks: Stop claiming the VDF/PoSW provides an age-floor that backstops immortality — a floor cannot kill an immortal message; only the TTL can. Keep the sequential token purely as an anti-spam cost. For freshness/anti-backdating, bind the token's nullifier to the per-session epoch_anchor that primitive A already mints: commit-reveal of BOTH phones' 128-bit session nonces + both ephemeral pubkeys. That anchor is a LOCAL, freshly-unpredictable challenge that exists without any global clock or beacon (so Invariant 1 holds) and without wire routing metadata (Invariant 2 holds). A blob originated in this session must carry a token spent against THIS session's anchor, so a precomputed-months-ago token cannot be re-bound to a fresh-looking origination event without re-running the (floored) sequential work bound to the new anchor — this is the token source's verbatim 'no-clock precompute defense.' Expiry stays entirely on the signed absolute TTL plus epoch_anchor freshness, which is the actual self-destruct mechanism.

fixes.title: Bind the sequential proof to a LOCAL fresh challenge (session epoch_anchor) so precompute/stockpiling cannot amortize the mint-cost at flood time
fixes.addresses: Precompute/stockpile amortization; N-device farm throughput (partial); verify-cost/battery DoS (partial).
fixes.howItWorks: A precomputed warehouse of blobs is worthless if each origination must spend a token whose nullifier is derived against the CURRENT session's commit-reveal anchor (both neighbors' fresh nonces + ephemeral pubkeys). The expensive sequential part can still be precomputed as a re-spendable token (compute never wasted — kept from the token source), but the FRESH per-session nullifier nf = H('nf' || s || epoch_anchor) cannot be precomputed before the session exists, and double-spend is caught by the local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set. This converts 'mint a warehouse, dump for free' into 'you still pay a fresh, un-precomputable spend per origination per neighbor,' which is the only economically meaningful gate available without a global beacon. Note origination has no counterparty handshake by default, so add a 'self-loop anchor' (from primitive D's self-loops) to give first-hop originations a fresh local anchor to bind against.

fixes.title: Make verification constant-time and fail-fast-uniform; cap per-session verify budget via the relay-token / diversity machinery to bound battery-DoS
fixes.addresses: Verify-cost asymmetry DoS; N-device farm battery exhaustion.
fixes.howItWorks: Choose the proof system so rejection cost ~= acceptance cost (Wesolowski-style short proof or a STARK with no early-exit asymmetry), and run verify in constant shape so a malformed proof cannot be made cheap-to-craft-but-expensive-to-reject. Then cap the number of NOVEL blobs a single PHY-radio neighbor can make you verify per local sliding epoch, reusing primitive A's distinct-PHY-radio diversity sketch and the per-neighbor cuckoo set: a flooding radio hits its verify budget and further blobs from it are dropped UNVERIFIED. This rate-limits the battery-DoS without any server, account, or targeting metadata (it's per-observed-radio, local-only). PHY-fingerprint linkage (~1.0, acknowledged by primitive D) is here a DEFENSIVE asset: it lets a victim attribute flood volume to a physical radio for local rate-limiting, even under sealed-sender.

fixes.title: If a sequential proof over the blob core is kept at all, hard-specify parameters and pin them in the build with an in-spec rationale; prefer hash-PoSW to sidestep discriminant risk entirely
fixes.addresses: Discriminant/parameter trap; ASIC constant-factor overstatement.
fixes.howItWorks: Should the project insist on a class-group VDF for the immutable-core binding, it MUST specify: discriminant = -p, p prime, p ≡ 3 (mod 4) (fundamental discriminant), |p| ≥ ~3300 bits for 128-bit security (published bound; ~830 bits is insufficient), with the discriminant derived verifiably-at-random (e.g., nothing-up-my-sleeve from a fixed seed) so no party can plant a low-order element. State the ASIC advantage honestly as ~10-31x (measured class-group squaring/XGCD speedups), NOT 'a small constant,' and size N so that even a 31x adversary's per-core rate is acceptable. Because there is no in-field rotation (Invariants 1, 6), treat the discriminant as a frozen, audited build constant. This fix is strictly inferior to fix #1 (hash-PoSW) for phones; offered only as a fallback so the parameter trap is closed if class groups are retained.

redteam.primitive: ORIGINATION-GATE (primitive D): receive-before-originate gate + Loopix Poisson outbound mixing + self-loops, targeting origination-event K-anonymity under assumed device-linkage approximately 1.0 (PHY fingerprinting). Spec sourced from C:\Users\rob\Documents\meldingx\docs\superpowers\specs\2026-06-25-polleneus-design.md section 10; threat model section 3; prior pass C:\Users\rob\Documents\meldingx\docs\superpowers\reviews\2026-06-25-redteam-v0.2.md.

attacks.title: Poisson mixing buys almost nothing once device-linkage = 1.0 (MixFlow / output-only correlation)
attacks.mechanism: Loopix Poisson delay protects against a GLOBAL adversary who must LINK an observed input to an observed output. But the spec's own threat model (section 3) concedes device-linkage approx 1.0: PHY/CFO-IQ radiometrics already label every emission with its source handset. The adversary therefore never needs to de-link input from output -- it reads the source label directly off the PHY of each advertisement. Poisson mixing of the OUTBOUND queue only randomizes WHICH of {real, relayed, dummy, self-loop} a given labeled device emits at a given instant; it does not hide that THIS device emitted SOMETHING. Worse, recent work (MixFlow, eprint 2023/199) correlates end-to-end mix flows at ~90% accuracy even at 60 cover packets/min and 50s Poisson delay -- and that is the HARD setting (global de-linking). Here the attacker has the easy setting (per-emission source labels for free). The anonymity therefore reduces entirely to the K-anonymity count from the gate, not the Poisson layer. Section 10's bullet 'timing reveals nothing about which blob is which' is true and irrelevant: the leak is per-device emission-event existence + first-appearance-in-mesh, not which blob.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 3 (anonymity via uniformity, real approx cover approx relayed): uniformity is a wire-byte property; it cannot survive a per-device PHY label that the design itself assumes. Poisson contributes no measurable anonymity beyond the gate's K, so the section-10 claim that mixing 'kills the origination timing tell' is overstated.

attacks.title: The gate does not bury originations at realistic K -- first-emergence + rumor-source estimation defeats it in sparse/medium venues
attacks.mechanism: The gate's premise: emit your real ID only after relaying >=k distinct novel IDs so your first-sighting graph looks like constant forwarding. But a multi-sensor mesh (threat model row 5) does not just see 'this device emits novelty' -- it reconstructs, per ID, the spatiotemporal spread tree (who first emitted it, then its neighbors, expanding outward). Classical rumor-source detection (Shah-Zaman rumor centrality on trees; Pinto-Thiran-Vetterli with sparse observers) estimates the source of an epidemic spread with high probability from a small fraction of timestamped first-sightings. For a RELAYED blob, your device is an interior node of someone else's spread tree (you appear AFTER others). For YOUR REAL blob, you are the ROOT: no upstream sighting exists anywhere in the mesh. The gate adds k relays of OTHERS' trees around you, but those are distinguishable spread-trees with distinct roots elsewhere; your real blob is still the one tree whose rumor-centrality estimator points at YOU with no upstream. The gate raises the attacker's work by a small additive constant (k extra trees to factor out), not by a multiplicative K. K = cover-originations / real-originations is the honest-cover ratio; it is NOT the same as resistance to source estimation on a labeled spread tree, where the discriminator is 'has an upstream sighting or not', a per-tree binary the gate does not erase.
attacks.breaks: The section-10 origination-event K-anonymity claim and threat-model row 5 ('countered by the gate, bounded to a measured K'). The realized anonymity set against a source-estimation adversary is far below the advertised K, and at low density approaches 1.

attacks.title: Gate self-deadlocks in exactly the sparse/blackout venues invariant 1 promises, and its fallback is itself a deanonymizing tell
attacks.mechanism: The gate requires relaying >=k distinct NOVEL IDs before originating. In a sparse venue or early blackout (low mean degree, near the percolation cliff per section 12.1) there IS little or no novelty to relay -- the whole reason novelty is scarce is that the mesh is thin. So a user who urgently needs to send (the protest/blackout use case, invariant 7) is GATED FROM SENDING precisely when it matters most: unbounded origination latency / starvation. Spec section 10 'degrades to a density-aware UX warning when novelty is scarce' converts this into a worse leak: the set of devices that originate DESPITE low local novelty is small and self-selected (people with something urgent to say), shrinking the anonymity set further AND temporally clustering originations against a quiet first-sighting background where any new root stands out maximally. The gate thus inverts: strong when you do not need it (dense), absent or actively harmful when you do (sparse). This is a direct collision between invariant 1 (full function in total blackout) and the gate's mechanism -- a sharper instance of the percolation tension the spec already flags, now weaponized.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 1 (full function in blackout): origination can stall indefinitely with no novelty. Invariant 7 (dense-gathering scope is the FLOOR; sparse onset of a gathering / thinning blackout still must function). Also undermines the section-10 K claim because the fallback shrinks K at the worst moment.

attacks.title: Self-loops are detectable and a precise active-probe / liveness oracle for the attacker
attacks.mechanism: Self-loops are blobs sealed to your OWN key that you emit and expect to see return. They are byte-uniform on the wire (good), but their BEHAVIORAL signature is not: a self-loop is an ID that ORIGINATES at device X and, uniquely, is TRIAL-DECRYPT-CONSUMED only when it returns to device X -- it never gets 'claimed' by any other device's inbox and never triggers a delete-token, yet device X demonstrably reacts to its return (it stops re-emitting that ID, or changes the reachability-UX state). A sensor mesh that already has per-device PHY labels can watch for IDs that (a) first appear at X and (b) whose circulation correlates with a state change only at X. More damaging: self-loops give the ACTIVE attacker a free reachability oracle. By selectively suppressing/jamming around a target and watching whether the target's behavior shifts to 'percolation failure' UX (self-loops stop returning -> the spec says this 'powers the honest density UX'), the attacker confirms it has isolated the target -- the n-1 attack the loops were meant to DETECT is also the attack they ENABLE the attacker to confirm. The loop is a two-way mirror.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 3 (uniformity): self-loops are wire-uniform but behaviorally distinguishable via the originate-here/consume-here-only pattern plus correlated state change, giving a side channel that singles out a device's own originations and its isolation status.

attacks.title: Cross-session intersection / statistical disclosure collapses K over repeated venue attendance
attacks.mechanism: K-anonymity is a per-session snapshot. With device-linkage approx 1.0, the SAME handset is re-identified across sessions (different days, different protests, same stadium block). Danezis-style statistical disclosure / intersection attacks (Information Hiding 2004; Kesdogan disclosure) need only the per-session SET of devices that originated at all, and the persistent association of a given recipient-friend's reception behavior. Over N sessions, intersecting 'which labeled devices were present-and-originating when friend F was reached' steadily narrows the sender set, because real friend-to-friend traffic is correlated (you message the same friend repeatedly) while cover is i.i.d. The gate and Poisson do nothing across sessions: K does not compound favorably, it ERODES -- each session is another observation reducing the long-run anonymity set toward 1. The spec treats K as a 'charge-independent floor' but never models the multi-session adversary, which is the realistic posture for a campus/recurring-protest (invariant 7) population.
attacks.breaks: The durability of the section-10 K-anonymity claim. K is presented as a floor; under intersection it is a per-session quantity that decays with repeated attendance, so the real long-run guarantee is weaker than advertised. (Does not break a hard invariant in one shot but defeats the property the primitive exists to provide.)

attacks.title: Token-gated relaying (selected source) shrinks the relay pool that the gate's K depends on -- coupled-failure
attacks.mechanism: Primitive D's gate needs ABUNDANT novel relays to build K; the selected token source (Reciprocity Hourglass) deliberately RATE-LIMITS relaying per neighbor per session and prices it (sequential PoSW / earned DAck credit). Honest low-resource phones that have not minted/earned tokens will relay LESS, so they accumulate the gate's k-novel-IDs threshold more slowly -> longer origination latency, and a SMALLER pool of cover-originations -> lower K for everyone. The two v0.3 primitives are in tension: the anti-abuse token throttle starves the anonymity gate of the very relay volume it converts into K. An adversary can amplify this by spending cheap cover-spends to consume honest neighbors' per-session relay slots (token exhaustion at the link), suppressing local relay throughput and thereby both raising origination latency and depressing K -- a low-cost anonymity-degradation + liveness attack that needs no PHY gear, just BLE proximity.
attacks.breaks: Liveness of invariant 1/7 in sparse-to-medium density and the magnitude of the section-10 K claim; an attacker degrades K by attacking the orthogonal token layer, showing the two primitives were not co-analyzed.

attacks.title: Poisson rate itself is a fingerprint and a covert origination-timing leak under adaptive multi-sensor analysis
attacks.mechanism: Loopix anonymity assumes a FIXED, public Poisson rate identical for all clients. The spec's density-adaptive auto-knobs (section 9) make cover floor / emission cadence a function of LOCALLY-observed density. Two devices in different micro-density pockets therefore emit at DIFFERENT Poisson rates -> the rate becomes a per-device/per-location fingerprint, and a device whose rate shifts is signaling its local density estimate (a coarse location/movement leak). Separately, even at fixed rate, an adaptive attacker that injects a KNOWN burst of novel IDs (active flooding) and watches a target's outbound queue can perform a flow-correlation against the known input pattern (the MixFlow setting) -- and because the gate ties origination eligibility to relayed-novelty count, the attacker controls the target's gate state: flood novelty -> target's gate opens -> the next root-with-no-upstream that appears at the target is disproportionately its real message. The attacker thus TRIGGERS originations to observe them.
attacks.breaks: Invariant 3 (uniformity): per-device adaptive Poisson rates are distinguishable and leak local density; the gate's coupling to attacker-controllable novelty lets an active adversary time and provoke real originations, partially defeating the timing protection the primitive claims.

fixes.title: Reframe and BOUND the claim: publish realized origination anonymity from a spread-tree source-estimator audit, not the cover ratio K
fixes.addresses: Attacks 2 and 5 (gate does not bury at realistic K; cross-session erosion). The cover-ratio K overstates resistance to source estimation.
fixes.howItWorks: Stop reporting K = cover/real as THE guarantee. Add a section-6/P6-style adversarial audit (alongside the planned USRP PHY self-audit) that runs an actual rumor-source estimator (Shah-Zaman rumor centrality; Pinto-Thiran-Vetterli sparse-observer MLE) against simulated and field first-sighting graphs at the target densities, and publishes the estimator's success probability and effective anonymity set as the headline number, in-app, the same way section 10 already commits to publishing the device-linkability number. Surface 'at THIS density your origination is identifiable with probability p' rather than 'hides among ~37'. This bends nothing; it is a truth-in-labeling extension of section 13's existing honesty stance.

fixes.title: Replace the hard receive-before-originate GATE with a probabilistic, time-bounded origination license that never deadlocks
fixes.addresses: Attack 3 (sparse-venue deadlock / starvation and the deanonymizing low-novelty fallback) and Attack 6 (token throttle starving the gate).
fixes.howItWorks: Convert the gate from a hard threshold (relay >=k novel before any origination) into a CONTINUOUS license that decays with a max latency cap: origination probability rises with relayed-novelty but is FLOORED above zero and CEILED in time, so a user is never blocked longer than a bounded T regardless of novelty. Critically, when novelty is scarce, do NOT degrade to a 'send anyway with a warning' (which clusters urgent senders); instead degrade to MANUFACTURING cover: the device emits its OWN self-loops and dummies to synthetically populate the local first-sighting background BEFORE releasing the real blob, so the real origination always appears against a non-empty root-set even in a thin mesh. This keeps invariant 1 (you can always eventually send in a blackout) and invariant 7 (functions at sparse onset), and removes the self-selected-urgent-sender tell. It bends invariant 3 slightly because in sparse mode a device emits more SELF-originated roots than relays -- but every such root is byte-uniform and the mix of real-vs-dummy among them is hidden, so per-blob uniformity holds; what changes is that K is now partly self-generated rather than borrowed from neighbors.

fixes.title: Make Poisson rate a fixed venue-wide constant (decouple cadence from density) and quantize any adaptation into a few public tiers
fixes.addresses: Attack 7 (adaptive per-device Poisson rate is a fingerprint and density leak) and partially Attack 1 (restores the Loopix uniform-rate precondition the mixing analysis depends on).
fixes.howItWorks: Loopix's anonymity proof requires identical Poisson rates across clients. Restore that precondition: fix the outbound emission rate to a single global constant for the BLE-only core, independent of locally-observed density. If battery-driven adaptation is unavoidable, quantize it to a SMALL number of publicly-defined tiers (e.g. 3) chosen by coarse, slowly-changing battery/buffer state with hysteresis, NOT by instantaneous neighbor count, so the rate is not a fine-grained location signal and the anonymity set is partitioned into at most a few large public classes rather than a continuum of per-device fingerprints. This keeps density-adaptation for the RETENTION/token-price knobs (section 9, where it is needed and harder to forge) but removes it from the one knob that doubles as a wire-timing fingerprint.

fixes.title: Treat self-loops as behaviorally suspicious: route loop-detection state through the SAME consume path as real inbox, and never expose an isolation-state UX that an attacker can probe
fixes.addresses: Attack 4 (self-loops are behaviorally distinguishable and form an attacker-usable isolation oracle).
fixes.howItWorks: Two changes. (1) Behavioral uniformity: make a returning self-loop trigger the IDENTICAL local state transitions as receiving any real message (it already trial-decrypts as 'mine'), and keep re-circulating a fraction of consumed self-loops exactly as the soup re-circulates already-read blobs, so 'originates-here-and-only-consumed-here' is no longer a clean pattern -- some real inbound also originates elsewhere-but-consumed-here, and some self-loops keep circulating after consumption. (2) Close the isolation oracle: the reachability/percolation UX must be derived with deliberate LAG and hysteresis and must never produce an externally-observable behavior change the instant loops stop returning; the device should keep emitting at the normal Poisson rate when isolated (cover does not drop just because the mesh is gone), so an attacker jamming around a target cannot read 'I have isolated you' off any timing or cadence change. The loop's defensive value (detecting that YOU are isolated, internally) is retained; its offensive value to the attacker (confirming isolation externally) is removed.

fixes.title: Add a cross-session linkage budget: rotate origination behavior and recipient-reception patterns to flatten intersection attacks
fixes.addresses: Attack 5 (statistical disclosure / intersection collapses K across repeated attendance).
fixes.howItWorks: Acknowledge that with device-linkage approx 1.0 the only lever left is making the per-session ORIGINATION decision statistically independent of the user's real send intent. Concretely: (a) decouple send-timing from intent by holding outbound real messages in the Poisson queue with a randomized session-scoped delay so 'F was reached shortly after device X attended' loses temporal correlation; (b) mandate that EVERY device that runs the app originates cover at a session-independent baseline whether or not the user has anything to say, so 'devices that originate' is not the self-selecting set the intersection attack feeds on (this is the standard receiver-bound/sender-cover defense from the SDA literature, Mallesh-Wright); (c) surface a 'recurring-venue linkage' warning in the red-team-reality UX (section 13) so users with high-stakes recurring patterns know to rotate identity (the panic/duress rotation in section 6 already exists). This bends nothing structurally; it raises the constant cover baseline (battery cost) and is honest about the residual.

fixes.title: Co-design the gate's k-threshold with the token relay budget so anti-abuse cannot starve anonymity
fixes.addresses: Attack 6 (token throttle and gate are in tension; attacker exhausts neighbor relay slots to depress K and raise latency).
fixes.howItWorks: Make the gate's novelty-counter credit RELAYED-AND-TOKEN-PAID novelty and RECEIVED novelty equally, so a device whose relaying is token-throttled still accrues gate credit from blobs it merely WITNESSES (trial-decrypts) without re-emitting -- decoupling 'eligible to originate' from 'has spent tokens to relay'. Separately, floor the per-neighbor relay budget (section 9 link tokens) high enough, and price cover-spends, such that an attacker cannot cheaply drain a neighbor's session relay slots purely to suppress throughput (the selected Hourglass source already floors N_relay > 0 and mandates cover-spends; extend that to a per-neighbor REJECTION-rate cap so a single peer cannot monopolize the slot budget). This requires the two primitives to share parameters (the density auto-knob in section 9 must jointly set token price AND gate-k), which the spec currently specifies independently. Preserves all invariants -- it is purely a parameter-coupling and crediting-rule change, no new wire metadata.

synthesis.tokenSourceVerdict: DENTED, not broken — survives as the right spine but the chosen instantiation does NOT survive its own red-team unmodified. The "Reciprocity Hourglass" (WRR spine + Hourglass hash-PoSW + ZK-nullifier) is correctly chosen: it is the only preserves-all candidate, has the best R7 (cheap mint path IS prosocial diverse relaying), lowest build cost (reuses §6/§8/§9), and the SWEAT path genuinely patches WRR's sparse-crowd liveness gap. But the red-team lands one CRITICAL hit that nullifies the primitive's reason to exist: the spend nullifier nf=H('nf'||s||epoch_anchor) is anchored per-neighbor-per-session, so by construction the SAME token yields a fresh valid nullifier against every distinct neighbor. With double-spend memory held only in a local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set (no global view, by invariant 6), one minted token buys ~D relay slots where D = distinct neighbors met — hundreds-to-thousands in a dense venue. The metering is therefore "one relay per token PER NEIGHBOR," which for a venue-mobile attacker is barely a limit. This is compounded, not independent: (a) cloud/botnet SWEAT-minting (sequentiality caps per-DEVICE rate, never device COUNT) produces tokens in embarrassing parallel, each then worth D slots; (b) the safety invariant "effective token cost STRICTLY ABOVE the slot value it buys" is mis-anchored — it was priced against ONE slot while the true value is D slots, so the discount-safety proof is over the wrong denominator; (c) the whole no-free-forge + unlinkability story funnels through one bespoke ZK wrapper (PoSW-accept in ZK) that is the least off-the-shelf code with no graceful degradation on a soundness bug. NET: the architecture choice is sound and worth keeping, but v0.4 MUST re-anchor the nullifier to the token (nf_token=H('nf'||s)) with epidemic gossip of seen-nullifiers, bound token TTL, re-price the discount against reachable-neighbors-per-token, and add a non-ZK per-PHY relay quota as defense-in-depth. With those four changes it survives; without the first one, primitive A provides essentially no rate-limit.

perPrimitive.primitive: A — Link-local relay tokens (Reciprocity Hourglass token source)
perPrimitive.topAttack: Per-neighbor nullifier anchor makes one token worth D relay slots: nf=H('nf'||s||epoch_anchor) yields a FRESH valid nullifier per distinct neighbor, and double-spend memory is only a local per-neighbor-per-session cuckoo set (no global view, inv 6). So a single minted (or cheaply cloud-SWEAT-minted) token buys ~D slots where D=distinct neighbors met (hundreds-thousands in a dense venue) — collapsing the metering the primitive exists to provide. The discount-safety rule 'cost > slot value' is mis-anchored by factor D.
perPrimitive.topFix: Re-anchor the spent-marker to the TOKEN not the neighbor: nf_token=H('nf'||s) (one per token, forever), keep a SEPARATE session-bound proof tag only to stop verbatim transcript replay, and gossip seen-nf_token sets epidemically in the same uniform fixed-length flood (carries no routing metadata → inv 2 holds; byte-uniform → inv 3 holds). This converts 'one slot per neighbor' to 'one slot per token venue-wide, modulo gossip-propagation delay,' shrinking the double-spend window from D to neighbors-reachable-in-one-gossip-epoch. Pair with token TTL (≤7d, signed, PoSW age-floor) and re-price the discount against expected-reachable-neighbors-per-token, not one slot.

perPrimitive.topAttack: Puncture-state growth is a feasibility wall on weak phones at venue scale: PE with negligible correctness error provably needs a key growing ≥ linearly in puncture count (Derler et al.); the only bound is Bloom-Filter Encryption, whose key is hundreds of MB up front (SafetyPin: BFE keys >64MB…>700MB on BLS12-381) on a 2-3GB phone already holding 1-2M blobs — OR non-BFE PE with 30s-to-minutes-per-decrypt linear in punctures. At stadium density the per-epoch puncture count is in the thousands within one ≤7d epoch, so it does not fit. BFE's escape (non-negligible FP) silently breaks 'never decryptable again' (FP retention) AND drops fresh mail (FN), undermining inv 1, 5, 7 and trial-decrypt reliability (inv 3).
perPrimitive.topFix: Replace puncture-on-READ with epoch-ratcheted forward-secure decryption (puncture-on-RECEIPT/on-TIME): a small fixed number of fine-grained sub-epochs inside the ≤7d TTL with HIBE-style forward key evolution driven by the existing gossip-median mesh clock (§6). Keys for elapsed sub-epochs are deleted whether or not the user read — killing the Message-Suppression Attack (seizer can't stop the clock) and the pre-read seizure window, and bounding key size to (sub-epochs remaining), a small constant. Per-message disappear-after-read becomes an OPTIONAL extra puncture, not the FS mechanism. Specify a concrete time-based BFKEM param set with a published low-end-Android key-size/cost benchmark as a P5/P6 release gate, plus two-layer key wrapping (key state encrypted under a StrongBox/SE wrapping key) so 'shred' is a hardware-honored crypto-erase, not a software delete on wear-leveled NAND.

perPrimitive.topAttack: The age-floor is a compute LOWER bound, not a wall-clock UPPER bound, so it cannot do the one job it claims ('backstops immortal-message/clock-skew'). N sequential iterations prove only that ≥T work happened SOMETIME before verification, over an ATTACKER-CHOSEN immutable core, with no external start-time anchor (inv 1 forbids a beacon). An adversary mints a blob today whose VDF 'proves' it is old (backdating), and a floor is the wrong direction to kill immortality — only the signed absolute TTL can expire a blob; the VDF adds zero expiry power. Compounded by precompute/stockpiling (no fresh per-use challenge → warehouse months of valid blobs, dump in a flood burst at ~zero marginal cost) and a direct contradiction with primitive A, which DELETES the class-group VDF (phones lack fast class-group arithmetic; ASIC gap is ~14-31x measured, not 'a small constant'; ~830-bit discriminants are insecure, ~3300 needed).
perPrimitive.topFix: Drop the standalone class-group VDF entirely; unify the mint-cost on primitive A's hash-based sequential PoSW (Cohen-Pietrzak/Poseidon) — hash hardware on phones, no group of unknown order, no discriminant single-point-of-failure, uniform cheap STARK verify for real/cover/relayed. Re-scope 'age-floor' to its only honest function (an anti-spam sequential cost + clock-skew sanity floor) and rely on the signed absolute TTL + epoch_anchor freshness for expiry/immortality. Defeat precompute by binding the per-origination spend nullifier to the per-session epoch_anchor (a local fresh challenge needing no beacon) and, for first-hop originations with no counterparty, to a self-loop anchor (primitive D). The expensive sequential token stays re-spendable (compute never wasted) but each spend needs a fresh, un-precomputable nullifier.

perPrimitive.topAttack: The gate self-deadlocks in exactly the sparse/blackout venues invariant 1 promises, and its fallback is itself a deanonymizing tell. The gate requires relaying ≥k distinct NOVEL IDs before originating, but in a thin/early-blackout mesh near the percolation cliff there IS little novelty to relay — so an urgent sender is GATED FROM SENDING precisely when it matters most (unbounded origination latency). The §10 'degrade to a density-aware UX warning and send anyway' fallback makes it worse: the set of devices that originate despite low local novelty is small, self-selected (people with something urgent), and temporally clusters real roots against a quiet first-sighting background where any new root stands out maximally — a direct inv 1 vs gate-mechanism collision, weaponized. Separately, Poisson mixing buys almost nothing once device-linkage≈1.0 (per-emission PHY source labels remove the need to de-link in/out), and the realized anonymity collapses to the gate's K, which rumor-source estimation on the labeled spread-tree drives toward 1 in sparse venues.
perPrimitive.topFix: Replace the HARD gate with a probabilistic, time-bounded origination license that never deadlocks (origination probability rises with relayed-novelty but is FLOORED >0 and CEILED at a max latency T). When novelty is scarce, do NOT 'send anyway with a warning' — instead MANUFACTURE cover: emit your own self-loops/dummies to synthetically populate the local first-sighting background BEFORE releasing the real blob, so a real origination always appears against a non-empty root-set even in a thin mesh (bends inv 3 slightly: more self-originated roots than relays in sparse mode, but every root is byte-uniform and real-vs-dummy is hidden). Also: report realized origination anonymity from a spread-tree source-estimator audit (rumor-centrality / Pinto-Thiran-Vetterli) as the headline number instead of the cover-ratio K; fix Poisson rate to a venue-wide constant (or ≤3 public tiers with hysteresis) to remove the per-device rate fingerprint; and credit gate eligibility from WITNESSED (trial-decrypted) novelty equally with relayed novelty so token-throttled phones aren't starved of origination eligibility.

specChanges.change: Re-specify the token-spend nullifier as nf_token=H('nf'||s) (ONE marker per token, lifetime-stable), decoupled from the neighbor, plus a separate session-bound proof tag that ties the transcript to THIS handshake (anti verbatim-replay only). Add epidemic gossip of locally-seen nf_token sets, piggybacked into the existing uniform flood as fixed-length opaque hashes (no routing metadata, byte-indistinguishable from blobs). State the residual double-spend window as 'neighbors reachable within one gossip-propagation epoch,' not D.

Replace the class-group VDF mint-cost with the hash-based sequential PoSW (Cohen-Pietrzak/Poseidon) that the selected token source already specifies, as the SINGLE token primitive. Delete the §9 'class-group VDF' bullet and the §10 'VDF pre-generation is fine' bullet's class-group dependence. Remove the spec's internal contradiction (two incompatible mint primitives in v0.3).

Bind a signed absolute TTL (≤7d, consistent with epoch-bucketing) INTO every TOKEN's committed core, not just blobs, with the PoSW iteration count as a clock-skew-resistant age-floor. Re-scope the 'age-floor backstops immortal-message' claim: state plainly that a sequential proof is a minimum-age FLOOR and CANNOT expire a message — expiry is entirely the signed TTL + epoch_anchor freshness. This kills the pre-mined war-chest dumped during a blackout.

Re-derive the discount-safety invariant against the CORRECT denominator: N_relay and N_sweat must satisfy cost > r × single-slot-value where r = expected reachable-neighbors-per-token under the new token-anchored nullifier (or, until that lands, an observable upper bound on D from the local diversity sketch). Specify the discount curve and clamp it using ONLY the local distinct-PHY estimate (no global oracle).

Replace puncture-on-READ with epoch-ratcheted forward-secure decryption (fine-grained time sub-epochs inside ≤7d, HIBE/time-based BFKEM, advanced on the §6 gossip-median clock). Make disappear-after-read an OPTIONAL extra puncture. Restate the crypto-shred guarantee as PROBABILISTIC ('decryptable after read with probability ≤ p' for a published p) and surface p in the §13 truth-in-labeling UX. Commit to a concrete time-based BFKEM parameter set with a hard per-sub-epoch puncture cap (forced ratchet on overflow) and a published low-end-Android key-size/cost benchmark as a release gate.

Mandate two-layer key wrapping: store the evolving/punctured key state encrypted under a wrapping key held in StrongBox/SE/TEE-backed Keystore (auth-bound, setUnlockedDeviceRequired). Puncture/ratchet rewrites only the encrypted blob; 'shred' and panic-wipe become destroy-the-SE-wrapping-key (hardware-honored crypto-erase, immune to wear-leveling/FTL NAND remnants). Surface in-app where SE/TEE is unavailable.

Gate and exactly-bind the delete-token: (1) require the same anti-abuse cost as any wire action (consumed against a link-local relay token / sequential spend per neighbor per session) to stop the unauthenticated flush-DoS firehose; (2) purge a held blob ONLY on an exact full-length match to a per-blob delete-tag committed INSIDE the sealed transcript (no loose/prefix match → no eviction of blobs the attacker didn't author); (3) add deletes (real + decoy) to primitive D's Poisson outbound queue to decorrelate emission timing from read events (closes the read-confirmation oracle); (4) honestly disclaim that a seizure of the secret retroactively links delete-token→message-ID.

Replace the hard receive-before-originate threshold with a probabilistic, time-bounded origination license (floored >0, ceiled at max latency T) and, in sparse mode, MANUFACTURE local cover (self-emit self-loops/dummies to populate the first-sighting background) before releasing the real blob — never 'send anyway with a warning.' Credit gate eligibility from WITNESSED (trial-decrypted) novelty equally with relayed novelty so token-throttled phones are not starved.

Stop reporting K=cover/real as THE guarantee. Add an adversarial spread-tree source-estimator audit (rumor-centrality; Pinto-Thiran-Vetterli sparse-observer MLE) on simulated + field first-sighting graphs and publish the estimator's success probability as the headline in-app number ('at this density your origination is identifiable with probability p'), alongside the planned USRP PHY self-audit. Model the multi-session intersection adversary explicitly: K is per-session and decays across repeated venue attendance.

Fix the outbound Poisson emission rate to a single venue-wide constant for the BLE core (restoring Loopix's identical-rate precondition); if battery adaptation is unavoidable, quantize to ≤3 public tiers driven by slow battery/buffer state with hysteresis, NOT instantaneous neighbor count, so the rate is not a per-device/location fingerprint. Keep density-adaptation on the token-price/retention knobs (§9) only.

Make a returning self-loop trigger the IDENTICAL local state transitions as any real inbound and keep re-circulating a fraction of consumed self-loops (so 'originate-here/consume-here-only' is not a clean behavioral signature). Derive the reachability/percolation UX with deliberate lag + hysteresis and keep emitting at normal Poisson rate when isolated, so an attacker jamming a target cannot read 'I have isolated you' off any cadence change (closes the isolation oracle the loops otherwise enable).

Add a non-ZK secondary local rate-limit independent of the token primitive: cap relay slots granted to any single observed PHY-radio-session to a small constant regardless of how many valid tokens it presents, and fail CLOSED to this quota if ZK verify exceeds a latency budget. Bounds a ZK-wrapper soundness bug to a leaf-level flood that uniformity tolerates instead of a venue-wide rate-limit collapse. Specify Fiat-Shamir transcript binding over the FULL statement (com, epoch_anchor, PoSW relation, domain separators) with a negative-test vector suite (Frozen-Heart class).

Promote to active research with concrete questions: (1) exact discount curve mapping diverse-novelty-served → N_relay that keeps cost provably above r×slot-value across the density range with no global oracle; (2) whether the ZK-spend wrapper verify is cheap enough on low-end Android (Poseidon vs Rescue vs Bulletproofs) against BLE MTU/battery; (3) N_sweat sizing so cloud/botnet SWEAT-minting is uneconomic at 50k-phone scale without honest battery harm — the core unresolved R1/R7 tension; (4) verify that SWEAT-minted vs RELAY-minted tokens are byte-indistinguishable at spend.

synthesis.biggestResidual: Funded-adversary device-COUNT, not the per-device cap, remains the dominant unresolved threat — and it is structurally unfixable without bending an invariant. Every per-device control the stack relies on (sequential PoSW, Poisson rate, diversity gate) bounds ONE device; none bounds how many devices a budgeted attacker brings or rents. Two faces: (1) CLOUD/BOTNET SWEAT-MINTING — sequentiality caps per-device mint rate but not core count, so ~10k rented sequential cores mint ~60k tokens/hr in parallel, each byte-indistinguishable from honest spends; the reciprocity 'attack-becomes-contribution' framing FAILS here because cloud nodes have no real crowd to serve, so they inject pure flood, and the no-global-difficulty-oracle invariant means N cannot be raised adaptively in response. (2) SPREAD-OUT FARM AT CROWD DENSITY — K real radios at K real locations each serving genuinely distinct strangers legitimately clears the diversity gate, mints the cheap reciprocity path, and is INDISTINGUISHABLE from K honest heavy users (the honorable O(crowd-size) hardware bound the spec already concedes). The token-anchored-nullifier fix (capping a token to ~1 venue-wide slot) is what keeps this from being catastrophic — it turns 'each token = D slots' back into 'each token ≈ 1 slot,' so a farm must pay sequential wall-clock per slot AND genuinely improve the mesh to mint cheaply. But the residual stands: a sufficiently funded adversary still floods at a cost it controls (rented cores) rather than one the venue controls (real crowd). The honest posture is the §1 promise restated precisely: this primitive makes flooding cost O(rented sequential cores + K physical radios), not impossible — and that bound, plus token TTL so stockpiles can't be pre-built, is the real, falsifiable guarantee. It is a dent, not a break, only because the architecture never claimed a true quota (inv 3/4 forbid one); it must be labeled, in-app, as a cost bound and not a wall.
synthesis.soulCheck: The soul holds, and the design's defining honesty is still its best feature — but v0.4 must apply that honesty to TWO overclaims this red-team exposed, or the running app will lie where the doc was careful. First overclaim: 'reading punctures the key so ciphertext can NEVER be decrypted again' (§5.2/§7) is FALSE under the only buildable instantiation (BFE has a non-negligible false-positive retention probability), and the underlying PE primitive may not even fit on a weak phone at venue scale. The fix is the project's own ethos: restate as 'decryptable after read with probability ≤ p,' surface p in the UX, and ratchet FS on TIME not on the user reading (which also closes the Message-Suppression seizure hole the §7 'a seized device can't recover read messages' line currently overpromises). Second overclaim: 'the VDF age-floor backstops immortal-message/clock-skew' (§9) is backwards — a floor proves minimum age and can never expire a message; only the signed TTL does that, and the class-group VDF contradicts the token source the same doc selected. The honest move is to delete the contradiction, unify on hash-PoSW, and say plainly that the sequential proof is an anti-spam cost, not a clock. Crucially the seven invariants are NOT bent by the recommended fixes: the token-anchored nullifier + gossiped seen-set carries opaque hashes only (inv 2/3 intact), token TTL reuses inv 5 machinery, time-ratcheted FS and SE-wrapped keys preserve inv 1/5/6, and the manufactured-cover origination license only bends inv 3 in sparse mode where every emitted root is still byte-uniform. The two truths §19 says to keep facing — the percolation cliff and the persistent sensor-net — both got SHARPER, not softer: the gate's sparse-venue self-deadlock IS the percolation cliff biting the anonymity layer, and device-linkage≈1.0 means the realized origination anonymity must be reported as a measured source-estimator probability, not the flattering cover-ratio K. State both in the app, replace 'never decryptable' and 'age-floor backstops immortality' with their honest probabilistic/floor-only versions, and the soul stays intact. Refusing to ship the two overclaims is the soul-check passing.

workflowProgress.promptPreview:
You are a cryptography + distributed-systems designer inventing a SYBIL-RESISTANT, ANONYMOUS, SERVERLESS token source for polleneus.


SEVEN INVARIANTS (any proposal must preserve all; label clearly if one is bent):
